{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /config/.claude/skills/sc-task-unified-protocol\n\n> **NOTE**: Classification has already been performed by the `/sc:task` command before this skill was invoked. The classification header has already been emitted. Do NOT emit it again. This skill handles **execution only** for STANDARD and STRICT tier tasks.\n>\n> If for any reason no classification header was emitted before this skill was invoked, emit one now using ONLY the tier values STRICT, STANDARD, LIGHT, or EXEMPT (no other values are valid).\n\n# /sc:task-unified - Unified Task Execution with Compliance\n\n## Purpose\n\nUnified command that merges orchestration capabilities with MCP compliance enforcement. Automatically classifies tasks into compliance tiers and enforces appropriate verification.\n\n**Key Benefits**:\n- Single command replaces confusion between `sc:task` and `sc:task-mcp`\n- Automatic tier classification with confidence scoring\n- Appropriate verification for each task type\n- Prevents over-engineering for trivial changes\n- Ensures safety for critical changes\n\n## Triggers\n\nUse `/sc:task` when:\n- Task involves code modifications with downstream impacts\n- Complexity score >0.6 with code modifications\n- Multi-file scope (>2 estimated affected files)\n- Security domain paths detected (auth/, security/, crypto/)\n- Refactoring or system-wide changes requested\n\n**Auto-Suggest Keywords**:\n- High confidence: \"implement feature\", \"refactor system\", \"fix security\", \"add authentication\", \"update database schema\"\n- Moderate confidence: \"add new\", \"create component\", \"update service\", \"modify API\"\n\n## Usage\n\n```bash\n/sc:task [description]                           # Auto-detect all dimensions\n/sc:task [description] --compliance strict       # Force STRICT tier\n/sc:task [description] --compliance light        # Force LIGHT tier\n/sc:task [description] --skip-compliance         # Bypass compliance (escape hatch)\n/sc:task [description] --verify auto             # Auto-select verification\n```\n\n## Behavioral Flow\n\n### 0. Classification (Already Performed)\n\nClassification was handled by the `/sc:task` command before this skill was invoked. The tier has been determined and the classification header has been emitted. Proceed directly to execution based on the classified tier.\n\n**Reference** — the tier keyword tables (for context only, do not re-classify):\n- **STRICT**: security, authentication, database, migration, refactor, breaking change, encrypt, token, session, oauth\n- **EXEMPT**: explain, search, commit, push, plan, discuss, brainstorm\n- **LIGHT**: typo, comment, whitespace, lint, docstring, formatting, minor\n- **STANDARD**: implement, add, create, update, fix, build, modify, change (default)\n\n### 2. Confidence Display (Human-Readable)\n\nAfter the mandatory classification header, show a human-readable summary:\n\n```\n**Tier: STANDARD** [████████░░] 80%\n\nClassified as STANDARD:\n- Keywords matched: add, implement\n- Confidence score: 0.78\n- Considered alternatives: STRICT (0.35)\n```\n\nIf confidence <70%, add prompt: \"⚠️ Low confidence. Override with: `--compliance [strict|standard|light|exempt]`\"\n\n**Note**: The machine-readable header (Section 0) is for telemetry/A/B testing. This human-readable display is for user understanding.\n\n### 3. Execution Phase\n\nExecute task according to tier requirements:\n\n**STRICT Execution**:\n1. Activate project (mcp__serena__activate_project)\n2. Verify git working directory clean (git status)\n3. Load codebase context (codebase-retrieval)\n4. Check relevant memories (list_memories -> read_memory)\n5. Identify all affected files and test files\n6. Make changes with full checklist\n7. Identify all files that import changed code\n8. Update all affected files\n9. Spawn verification agent (quality-engineer)\n10. Run comprehensive tests: `pytest [path] -v`\n11. Answer adversarial questions\n\n**STANDARD Execution**:\n1. Load context via codebase-retrieval\n2. Search downstream impacts (find_referencing_symbols OR grep)\n3. Make changes\n4. Run affected tests OR document manual verification\n5. Verify basic functionality\n\n**LIGHT Execution**:\n1. Quick scope check (files/lines within bounds)\n2. Make changes\n3. Quick sanity check (syntax valid, no obvious errors)\n4. Proceed with judgment\n\n**EXEMPT Execution**:\n1. Execute immediately\n2. No verification overhead\n\n### 4. Verification Phase\n\nRoute to appropriate verification based on tier and paths:\n\n| Compliance Tier | Verification Method | Token Cost | Timeout |\n|-----------------|---------------------|------------|---------|\n| STRICT | Sub-agent (quality-engineer) | 3-5K | 60s |\n| STANDARD | Direct test execution | 300-500 | 30s |\n| LIGHT | Skip verification | 0 | 0s |\n| EXEMPT | Skip verification | 0 | 0s |\n\n**Critical Path Override**: Paths matching `auth/`, `security/`, `crypto/`, `models/`, `migrations/` always trigger CRITICAL verification regardless of compliance tier.\n\n**Trivial Path Override**: Paths matching `*.md`, `docs/`, `*test*.py` may skip verification.\n\n### 4.5 Test Failure Escalation Protocol (TFEP)\n\n**CRITICAL**: When tests fail during the Verification Phase (Section 4), agents MUST follow this protocol. Ad-hoc fixes are PROHIBITED.\n\n#### TFEP Prohibition Rules (VIOLATION-level)\n\nThese rules apply to ALL compliance tiers that run tests (STRICT, STANDARD):\n\n1. **VIOLATION**: Agents MUST NOT fix any code in response to test failures without completing the TFEP workflow.\n2. **VIOLATION**: Agents MUST NOT modify test expectations to make failing tests pass without adversarial validation.\n3. **VIOLATION**: Ad-hoc patches derived from test output are PROHIBITED. The agent must not read a traceback and immediately edit code to resolve it.\n\n**Permitted Exceptions** (MAY fix directly without TFEP):\n- Single `ImportError`/`NameError` in test scaffolding the agent just wrote, affecting <=2 tests, where the error is in the test file itself (not in implementation code).\n- Lint/formatting failures (trivially fixable, unambiguous root cause).\n- Deprecation warnings (not failures).\n\n**Valid Adversarial Outcome**: \"Test expectations are wrong\" is a legitimate conclusion from adversarial debate. When the debate concludes that test expectations are outdated rather than the code being wrong, this is NOT grounds for the agent to directly edit the tests — it must be presented to the user for review.\n\n#### Test Baseline Snapshot (Pre-Implementation)\n\nBefore implementation begins, the agent MUST record a test baseline:\n\n1. Capture the list of all existing test files and test function names at task start (e.g., via `uv run pytest --collect-only -q` or directory listing).\n2. Store this baseline in memory for the duration of the task.\n3. On any test failure, classify each failing test as:\n   - **Pre-existing**: Test name appears in the baseline (existed before this task).\n   - **New**: Test name does NOT appear in the baseline (written by the agent during this task).\n4. This classification drives the MUST-escalate vs MAY-fix-directly decision below.\n\n#### Escalation Trigger Detection\n\n**MUST escalate (trigger TFEP):**\n- Any **pre-existing test** fails (tests in the baseline that were not modified by the agent). This is the primary trigger — regressions in existing tests indicate the implementation broke something.\n- **3 or more new tests** fail simultaneously (indicates a systemic issue, not a simple typo).\n- **Runtime exceptions in implementation code** (TypeError, AttributeError, KeyError, etc. in the code being tested, not in the test scaffolding itself).\n\n**Escalation gradient (within-TFEP, for future forensic integration):**\n- Repeated failure (same test cluster fails after fix attempt) → escalate from light to standard\n- Multi-file blast radius from recent changes → escalate\n- Low-confidence root cause from adversarial debate → escalate\n- Unresolved adversarial outcome (no winner/tie) → escalate\n- Second failed retest → escalate\n- Cross-domain or non-obvious regression → escalate\n\n#### TFEP Execution Flow\n\nWhen TFEP triggers, execute the following steps:\n\n**Step 1: Halt and freeze**\n1. **STOP** testing immediately.\n2. **FREEZE** implementation — no further code changes permitted.\n\n**Step 2: Construct failure context**\n3. Build a `failure_context` YAML package containing:\n   - `test_names`: list of failing test function names\n   - `test_files`: list of test files containing failures\n   - `error_output`: full traceback/error output\n   - `expected_behavior`: what was expected\n   - `actual_behavior`: what actually happened\n   - `changes_made`: files changed during this task with descriptions\n   - `task_description`: current task description\n   - `test_baseline`: the pre-implementation test baseline\n   - `escalation_count`: which TFEP trigger this is (1, 2, or 3)\n4. Write context to `{output_dir}/context.yaml`.\n\n**Step 3: Invoke forensic**\n5. Determine the forensic tier based on escalation count:\n   - 1st trigger → `--tier light --intent triage`\n   - 2nd trigger → `--tier standard`\n   - 3rd trigger → **FULL STOP**. Report to user. Do not attempt further fixes.\n6. Invoke: `/sc:forensic --tier {tier} --intent triage --caller task-unified --context {context_path} --output {output_dir} --depth quick`\n7. The forensic pipeline runs autonomously through all its phases and returns a structured return contract.\n\n**Step 4: Consume forensic results**\n8. Read the forensic return contract from `{output_dir}/return-contract.yaml`.\n9. Handle based on status:\n   - If `test_is_wrong == true`: Present to user for review. Do NOT auto-fix tests.\n   - If `status == \"success\"`: Proceed to Step 5 (tasklist insertion).\n   - If `status == \"partial\"` or `recommended_escalation != \"none\"`: Increment `escalation_count` and return to Step 3.\n   - If `status == \"failed\"`: Report to user, halt execution.\n\n**Step 5: Tasklist insertion**\n10. Read `tasklist_insertion_path` from the return contract.\n11. Insert the remediation block into the current tasklist:\n    - Add `## Failure Remediation Plan (Adjudicated)` heading.\n    - Insert remediation tasks BEFORE existing test/verification tasks.\n    - Preserve original tasklist structure — append, don't replace.\n\n**Step 6: Resume**\n12. Resume execution with `--compliance strict` starting from the inserted remediation tasks.\n13. After remediation tasks complete, re-run the original test suite.\n14. If tests pass: TFEP resolved successfully. Produce incident report and continue.\n15. If tests fail again: Increment `escalation_count`, return to Step 2.\n\n#### TFEP Incident Reporting\n\nAfter each TFEP resolution (success or escalation), produce `tfep-incident-report.md`:\n\n```markdown\n# TFEP Incident Report\n\n- **Trigger**: {which threshold rule fired}\n- **Escalation count**: {1, 2, or 3}\n- **Failing tests**: {test names and classification}\n- **Root cause**: {summary from rca-verdict.md}\n- **Solution**: {summary from solution-verdict.md}\n- **Outcome**: {success / escalated / failed}\n- **Forensic artifacts**: {path to output_dir}\n```\n\nThis report is committed to git alongside other forensic artifacts.\n\n#### Escalation Budget\n\n```\n1st TFEP trigger  → /sc:forensic --tier light --intent triage    (~5-8K tokens)\n2nd TFEP trigger  → /sc:forensic --tier standard                 (~15-20K tokens)\n3rd TFEP trigger  → FULL STOP. Report to user. Do not attempt further fixes.\n```\n\n### 5. Feedback Collection\n\nAfter completion, collect implicit feedback:\n- Track if user overrode tier (implicit classification feedback)\n- Note smooth completion vs errors (quality signal)\n- Store for calibration learning\n\n## MCP Integration\n\n**Required Servers by Tier**:\n- STRICT: Sequential, Serena (fallback not allowed)\n- STANDARD: Sequential, Context7 (fallback allowed)\n- LIGHT: None required (fallback allowed)\n- EXEMPT: None required\n\n**Circuit Breaker Behavior**:\n- If required servers unavailable for STRICT tier, block task execution\n- For other tiers, use fallbacks with noted limitations\n\n## Tool Coordination\n\n**Planning Phase**:\n1. TodoWrite: Create task breakdown\n2. codebase-retrieval: Load context\n3. list_memories / read_memory: Check project state\n\n**Execution Phase**:\n1. Edit/MultiEdit/Write: Make changes\n2. Grep/Glob: Find references\n3. find_referencing_symbols: Trace dependencies\n\n**Verification Phase**:\n1. Task (quality-engineer): Spawn verification agent (STRICT only)\n2. Bash: Run tests directly (STANDARD)\n3. think_about_task_adherence: Reflect on completeness\n\n**Completion Phase**:\n1. write_memory: Save session state\n2. think_about_whether_you_are_done: Final check\n\n## Examples\n\n### STRICT Task\n```\n/sc:task \"implement user authentication with JWT\"\n\n-> Classified as STRICT (security domain, authentication keyword)\n-> Full 6-category checklist enforced\n-> Verification agent spawned\n-> Adversarial questions answered\n```\n\n### STANDARD Task\n```\n/sc:task \"add pagination to user list\"\n\n-> Classified as STANDARD (add keyword, typical feature)\n-> Context loaded before editing\n-> Downstream impacts checked\n-> Direct test execution\n```\n\n### LIGHT Task\n```\n/sc:task \"fix typo in README\"\n\n-> Classified as LIGHT (trivial keyword, documentation path)\n-> Quick sanity check only\n-> No verification delay\n```\n\n### EXEMPT Task\n```\n/sc:task \"explain how the auth flow works\"\n\n-> Classified as EXEMPT (explain pattern detected)\n-> Immediate execution\n-> No compliance overhead\n```\n\n### Override Example\n```\n/sc:task \"update config file\" --compliance strict\n\n-> User override to STRICT regardless of classification\n-> Full verification enforced\n```\n\n## Boundaries\n\n**Will:**\n- Classify tasks into appropriate compliance tiers\n- Enforce tier-appropriate verification requirements\n- Provide confidence scoring with rationale\n- Track feedback for continuous calibration\n- Support user overrides with justification\n\n**Will Not:**\n- Skip safety-critical verification for STRICT tasks\n- Apply STRICT overhead to genuinely trivial changes\n- Override user's explicit compliance choice\n- Proceed with <70% confidence without user confirmation\n\n## Success Criteria\n\n| Metric | Target | Measurement |\n|--------|--------|-------------|\n| Tier classification accuracy | >=80% | User feedback on appropriateness |\n| User confusion rate | <10% | \"Which command?\" questions eliminated |\n| Skip rate (--skip-compliance) | <12% | Override tracking |\n| Regression prevention | >=85% | Post-verification bug detection |\n| STRICT tier overhead | <25% | Execution telemetry |\n\n## Configuration References\n\n- Keywords: `config/tier-keywords.yaml`\n- Verification routing: `config/verification-routing.yaml`\n- Acceptance criteria: `config/tier-acceptance-criteria.yaml`\n- gate criteria -- data definitions for each pipeline step's gate.\n\nThis module defines GateCriteria instances as module-level constants.\nGate criteria are pure data -- no logic, no imports from pipeline/gates.py\nenforcement code (NFR-005).\n\nSemantic check functions are defined here as pure functions accepting\nfile content and returning bool. They are registered on the STRICT-tier\nGateCriteria instances.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom ..audit.wiring_gate import WIRING_GATE\nfrom ..pipeline.models import GateCriteria, SemanticCheck\n\n# --- Semantic check functions (pure: content -> bool) ---\n\n\ndef _no_heading_gaps(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Verify heading levels increment by at most 1 (no H2 -> H4 skip).\"\"\"\n    prev_level = 0\n    for line in content.splitlines():\n        stripped = line.lstrip()\n        if stripped.startswith(\"#\"):\n            level = 0\n            for ch in stripped:\n                if ch == \"#\":\n                    level += 1\n                else:\n                    break\n            if prev_level > 0 and level > prev_level + 1:\n                return False\n            prev_level = level\n    return True\n\n\ndef _cross_refs_resolve(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Verify all 'See section' / cross-reference patterns have matching headings.\n\n    Checks that internal references like 'See section X.Y' or 'See X.Y'\n    have corresponding headings. Unresolved references emit warnings but\n    do not block the pipeline (warning-only mode per OQ-001 for v2.20).\n    \"\"\"\n    import re\n    import warnings\n\n    # Extract heading anchors (simplified: heading text, lowercased)\n    headings: set[str] = set()\n    for line in content.splitlines():\n        stripped = line.lstrip()\n        if stripped.startswith(\"#\"):\n            heading_text = stripped.lstrip(\"#\").strip().lower()\n            headings.add(heading_text)\n\n    # Find cross-references like \"See section X\" or \"(see X.Y)\"\n    refs = re.findall(r'[Ss]ee\\s+(?:[Ss]ection\\s+)?[\"\\']?(\\d+(?:\\.\\d+)*)', content)\n    # If there are no cross-references, that's fine\n    if not refs:\n        return True\n\n    # Check each reference against headings\n    unresolved: list[str] = []\n    for ref in refs:\n        found = any(ref in h for h in headings)\n        if not found:\n            # Also check if the section number appears as a heading prefix\n            found = any(h.startswith(ref) for h in headings)\n        if not found:\n            unresolved.append(ref)\n\n    if unresolved:\n        for ref in unresolved:\n            warnings.warn(\n                f\"Unresolved cross-reference: 'See section {ref}' has no matching heading\",\n                stacklevel=2,\n            )\n        # Warning-only mode (OQ-001): return True to avoid blocking pipeline\n        return True\n\n    return True\n\n\ndef _no_duplicate_headings(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"No duplicate H2 or H3 heading text.\"\"\"\n    seen: dict[int, set[str]] = {2: set(), 3: set()}\n    for line in content.splitlines():\n        stripped = line.lstrip()\n        if stripped.startswith(\"## \") and not stripped.startswith(\"### \"):\n            text = stripped[3:].strip().lower()\n            if text in seen[2]:\n                return False\n            seen[2].add(text)\n        elif stripped.startswith(\"### \"):\n            text = stripped[4:].strip().lower()\n            if text in seen[3]:\n                return False\n            seen[3].add(text)\n    return True\n\n\ndef _frontmatter_values_non_empty(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"All YAML frontmatter fields have non-empty values.\"\"\"\n    fm = _parse_frontmatter(content)\n    if fm is None:\n        return False\n    for _key, value in fm.items():\n        if not value.strip():\n            return False\n    return True\n\n\ndef _has_actionable_content(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"At least one section contains numbered or bulleted items.\"\"\"\n    import re\n\n    # Look for markdown list items: \"- \", \"* \", \"1. \", \"2. \", etc.\n    return bool(re.search(r\"^\\s*(?:[-*]|\\d+\\.)\\s+\\S\", content, re.MULTILINE))\n\n\ndef _strip_yaml_quotes(value: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Strip matching outer YAML quotes (single or double) from a value.\n\n    Handles the common case where LLMs wrap values in quotes:\n      '\"1:1\"'  -> '1:1'\n      \"'1:1'\"  -> '1:1'\n\n    Does NOT strip if quotes are unmatched:\n      '\"1:1'   -> '\"1:1'   (unmatched -- leave as-is)\n    \"\"\"\n    if len(value) >= 2:\n        if (value[0] == '\"' and value[-1] == '\"') or (\n            value[0] == \"'\" and value[-1] == \"'\"\n        ):\n            return value[1:-1]\n    return value\n\n\ndef _parse_frontmatter(content: str) -> dict[str, str] | None:\n    \"\"\"Extract YAML frontmatter key-value pairs from content.\n\n    Returns a dict of key→value strings, or None if no frontmatter found.\n    Values are stripped of whitespace and matching outer YAML quotes.\n    \"\"\"\n    stripped = content.lstrip()\n    if not stripped.startswith(\"---\"):\n        return None\n\n    rest = stripped[3:].lstrip(\"\\n\")\n    end_idx = rest.find(\"\\n---\")\n    if end_idx == -1:\n        return None\n\n    result: dict[str, str] = {}\n    for line in rest[:end_idx].splitlines():\n        line = line.strip()\n        if \":\" in line:\n            key, value = line.split(\":\", 1)\n            result[key.strip()] = _strip_yaml_quotes(value.strip())\n    return result\n\n\ndef _high_severity_count_zero(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Validate that high_severity_count equals zero in fidelity report frontmatter.\n\n    Returns True only if the frontmatter contains high_severity_count with\n    integer value 0. Returns False if:\n    - Frontmatter is missing\n    - high_severity_count field is missing\n    - high_severity_count > 0\n\n    Raises TypeError if high_severity_count value is not parseable as an integer.\n    \"\"\"\n    fm = _parse_frontmatter(content)\n    if fm is None:\n        return False\n\n    value = fm.get(\"high_severity_count\")\n    if value is None:\n        return False\n\n    try:\n        count = int(value)\n    except (ValueError, TypeError):\n        raise TypeError(f\"high_severity_count must be an integer, got: {value!r}\")\n\n    return count == 0\n\n\ndef _has_per_finding_table(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Verify the certification report contains a per-finding results table.\n\n    Checks for a markdown table with the required columns:\n    Finding | Severity | Result | Justification\n    \"\"\"\n    import re\n\n    # Look for the table header row with required columns\n    has_header = bool(\n        re.search(\n            r\"\\|\\s*Finding\\s*\\|\\s*Severity\\s*\\|\\s*Result\\s*\\|\\s*Justification\\s*\\|\",\n            content,\n            re.IGNORECASE,\n        )\n    )\n    # Also check for at least one data row (| F-XX | ... |)\n    has_data = bool(\n        re.search(\n            r\"\\|\\s*F-\\d+\\s*\\|\",\n            content,\n        )\n    )\n    return has_header and has_data\n\n\ndef _all_actionable_have_status(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Verify all non-SKIPPED entries in remediation tasklist have FIXED or FAILED status.\n\n    Scans for checklist entries matching `- [ ] F-XX | file | STATUS -- desc`\n    and ensures STATUS is one of FIXED or FAILED (not PENDING).\n    SKIPPED entries (marked `- [x]`) are excluded from the check.\n\n    Returns True if all actionable entries have terminal status, or if there\n    are no actionable entries. Returns False if any PENDING entry is found.\n    \"\"\"\n    import re\n\n    # Match actionable (unchecked) entries: - [ ] F-XX | file | STATUS -- desc\n    actionable_pattern = re.compile(\n        r\"^-\\s+\\[ \\]\\s+F-\\d+\\s*\\|[^|]*\\|\\s*(\\w+)\\s*--\",\n        re.MULTILINE,\n    )\n    for match in actionable_pattern.finditer(content):\n        status = match.group(1).upper()\n        if status not in (\"FIXED\", \"FAILED\"):\n            return False\n    return True\n\n\ndef _tasklist_ready_consistent(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Validate tasklist_ready is consistent with severity counts.\n\n    Consistency rule: tasklist_ready=true requires high_severity_count=0\n    AND validation_complete=true. If tasklist_ready=true but either\n    condition fails, this returns False (inconsistency detected).\n\n    Returns False if:\n    - Frontmatter is missing\n    - tasklist_ready field is missing\n    - Inconsistency: tasklist_ready=true but high_severity_count > 0\n    - Inconsistency: tasklist_ready=true but validation_complete != true\n    \"\"\"\n    fm = _parse_frontmatter(content)\n    if fm is None:\n        return False\n\n    tasklist_ready_str = fm.get(\"tasklist_ready\")\n    if tasklist_ready_str is None:\n        return False\n\n    tasklist_ready = tasklist_ready_str.lower() == \"true\"\n\n    if not tasklist_ready:\n        # If tasklist_ready is false, that's always consistent\n        return True\n\n    # tasklist_ready is true -- verify consistency\n    high_str = fm.get(\"high_severity_count\")\n    if high_str is None:\n        return False\n    try:\n        high_count = int(high_str)\n    except (ValueError, TypeError):\n        return False\n\n    if high_count > 0:\n        return False\n\n    validation_str = fm.get(\"validation_complete\")\n    if validation_str is None:\n        return False\n    if validation_str.lower() != \"true\":\n        return False\n\n    return True\n\n\ndef _convergence_score_valid(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"convergence_score frontmatter value parses as float in [0.0, 1.0].\"\"\"\n    fm = _parse_frontmatter(content)\n    if fm is None:\n        return False\n    value = fm.get(\"convergence_score\")\n    if value is None:\n        return False\n    try:\n        score = float(value)\n        return 0.0 <= score <= 1.0\n    except (ValueError, TypeError):\n        return False\n\n\n# --- DEVIATION_ANALYSIS_GATE semantic check functions ---\n\n\ndef _no_ambiguous_deviations(content: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Fail-closed: ambiguous_deviations must equal 0.\n\n    Returns True only if frontmatter contains ambiguous_deviations: 0.\n    Returns False for:\n    - return their contents as fenced code blocks.\n    70→\n    71→    Each file is wrapped in a fenced block with a ``# <path>`` header.\n    72→    Returns an empty string when *input_paths* is empty (no-op).\n    73→    \"\"\"\n    74→    if not input_paths:\n    75→        return \"\"\n    76→\n    77→    blocks: list[str] = []\n    78→    for p in input_paths:\n    79→        content = Path(p).read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    80→        blocks.append(f\"# {p}\\n```\\n{content}\\n```\")\n    81→    return \"\\n\\n\".join(blocks)\n    82→\n    83→\n    84→def _sanitize_output(output_file: Path) -> int:\n    85→    \"\"\"Strip conversational preamble and leading blank lines before YAML\n    86→    frontmatter in a step output file.\n    87→\n    88→    Reads the file, strips leading blank lines, finds the first ``^---``\n    89→    line, and removes everything before it.  Uses atomic write (write to\n    90→    ``.tmp`` then ``os.replace()``) to prevent partial file states.\n    91→\n    92→    Returns the byte count of the stripped content (0 when file already\n    93→    starts with ``---`` and has no preamble).\n    94→    \"\"\"\n    95→    import os\n    96→    import re\n    97→\n    98→    try:\n    99→        raw = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   100→    except FileNotFoundError:\n   101→        return 0\n   102→\n   103→    # Strip leading blank lines (LLMs sometimes emit \\n\\n before ---)\n   104→    content = raw.lstrip(\"\\n\\r\\t \")\n   105→\n   106→    # Already starts with frontmatter delimiter -- no preamble to strip\n   107→    if content.startswith(\"---\"):\n   108→        if content == raw:\n   109→            return 0\n   110→        # Leading whitespace only -- write back the stripped version\n   111→        preamble_bytes = len(raw.encode(\"utf-8\")) - len(content.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n   112→        tmp_file = output_file.with_suffix(output_file.suffix + \".tmp\")\n   113→        tmp_file.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   114→        os.replace(tmp_file, output_file)\n   115→        _log.info(\n   116→            \"Stripped %d-byte leading whitespace from %s\",\n   117→            preamble_bytes,\n   118→            output_file,\n   119→        )\n   120→        return preamble_bytes\n   121→\n   122→    # Search for the first ^--- line (conversational preamble case)\n   123→    match = re.search(r\"^---[ \\t]*$\", content, re.MULTILINE)\n   124→    if match is None:\n   125→        # No frontmatter found at all -- leave file unchanged\n   126→        return 0\n   127→\n   128→    preamble = content[: match.start()]\n   129→    cleaned = content[match.start():]\n   130→    # Total bytes stripped = leading whitespace + conversational preamble\n   131→    preamble_bytes = len(raw.encode(\"utf-8\")) - len(cleaned.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n   132→\n   133→    # Atomic write: tmp file + os.replace\n   134→    tmp_file = output_file.with_suffix(output_file.suffix + \".tmp\")\n   135→    tmp_file.write_text(cleaned, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   136→    os.replace(tmp_file, output_file)\n   137→\n   138→    _log.info(\"Stripped %d-byte preamble from %s\", preamble_bytes, output_file)\n   139→    return preamble_bytes\n   140→\n   141→\n   142→def _inject_pipeline_diagnostics(\n   143→    output_file: Path,\n   144→    started_at: datetime,\n   145→    finished_at: datetime,\n   146→) -> None:\n   147→    \"\"\"Inject executor-populated pipeline_diagnostics into extraction frontmatter.\n   148→\n   149→    The LLM cannot reliably produce execution timing or environment metadata,\n   150→    so the executor injects these fields post-subprocess (FR-033).\n   151→    \"\"\"\n   152→    content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").lstrip(\"\\n\\r\\t \")\n   153→    if not content.startswith(\"---\"):\n   154→        return\n   155→\n   156→    # Find end of frontmatter\n   157→    end_idx = content.find(\"\\n---\", 3)\n   158→    if end_idx == -1:\n   159→        return\n   160→\n   161→    # Idempotency: skip if already injected\n   162→    frontmatter = content[3:end_idx]\n   163→    if \"pipeline_diagnostics:\" in frontmatter:\n   164→        return\n   165→\n   166→    elapsed = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n   167→    diagnostics_line = (\n   168→        f\"pipeline_diagnostics: \"\n   169→        f\"{{elapsed_seconds: {elapsed:.1f}, \"\n   170→        f'started_at: \"{started_at.isoformat()}\", '\n   171→        f'finished_at: \"{finished_at.isoformat()}\"}}'\n   172→    )\n   173→\n   174→    # Insert before the closing ---\n   175→    new_content = content[:end_idx] + \"\\n\" + diagnostics_line + content[end_idx:]\n   176→    output_file.write_text(new_content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   177→\n   178→\n   179→def _inject_provenance_fields(\n   180→    output_file: Path,\n   181→    spec_source: str,\n   182→) -> None:\n   183→    \"\"\"Inject provenance fields into test-strategy frontmatter.\n   184→\n   185→    The test-strategy step's LLM output may omit provenance metadata\n   186→    (spec_source, generated, generator) since the prompt focuses on\n   187→    test strategy content. The executor injects these fields post-subprocess\n   188→    to satisfy TEST_STRATEGY_GATE required_frontmatter_fields.\n   189→    \"\"\"\n   190→    content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").lstrip(\"\\n\\r\\t \")\n   191→    if not content.startswith(\"---\"):\n   192→        return\n   193→\n   194→    # Find end of frontmatter\n   195→    end_idx = content.find(\"\\n---\", 3)\n   196→    if end_idx == -1:\n   197→        return\n   198→\n   199→    # Idempotency: only inject fields that are missing\n   200→    frontmatter = content[3:end_idx]\n   201→\n   202→    fields_to_inject = []\n   203→    if \"spec_source:\" not in frontmatter:\n   204→        fields_to_inject.append(f\"spec_source: {spec_source}\")\n   205→    if \"generated:\" not in frontmatter:\n   206→        generated = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n   207→        fields_to_inject.append(f'generated: \"{generated}\"')\n   208→    if \"generator:\" not in frontmatter:\n   209→        fields_to_inject.append(\"generator: superclaude-roadmap-executor\")\n   210→\n   211→    if not fields_to_inject:\n   212→        return  # Wiring-verification: run static analysis directly, no Claude subprocess.\n   242→    # Returns PASS unconditionally; gate evaluation is handled separately by\n   243→    # the trailing gate runner (section 5.7.1).\n   244→    if step.id == \"wiring-verification\":\n   245→        from ..audit.wiring_gate import run_wiring_analysis, emit_report\n   246→        from ..audit.wiring_config import WiringConfig\n   247→\n   248→        wiring_config = WiringConfig(rollout_mode=\"soft\")\n   249→        source_dir = config.output_dir.parent if hasattr(config, 'output_dir') else Path(\".\")\n   250→        report = run_wiring_analysis(wiring_config, source_dir)\n   251→        step.output_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n   252→        emit_report(report, step.output_file)\n   253→        return StepResult(\n   254→            step=step,\n   255→            status=StepStatus.PASS,\n   256→            attempt=1,\n   257→            started_at=started_at,\n   258→            finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n   259→        )\n   260→\n   261→    # Inline embedding: read input files into the prompt instead of --file flags\n   262→    # --file is broken (cloud download mechanism, not local file injector) so\n   263→    # inline embedding is always used regardless of composed prompt size.\n   264→    embedded = _embed_inputs(step.inputs)\n   265→    if embedded:\n   266→        composed = step.prompt + \"\\n\\n\" + embedded\n   267→        if len(composed.encode(\"utf-8\")) > _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT:\n   268→            # <= is intentional; _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT = 120 KB is safely below MAX_ARG_STRLEN = 128 KB\n   269→            _log.warning(\n   270→                \"Step '%s': composed prompt exceeds %d bytes; embedding inline anyway\"\n   271→                \" (--file fallback is unavailable)\",\n   272→                step.id,\n   273→                _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT,\n   274→            )\n   275→        effective_prompt = composed\n   276→        extra_args: list[str] = []\n   277→    else:\n   278→        effective_prompt = step.prompt\n   279→        extra_args = []\n   280→\n   281→    proc = ClaudeProcess(\n   282→        prompt=effective_prompt,\n   283→        output_file=step.output_file,\n   284→        error_file=step.output_file.with_suffix(\".err\"),\n   285→        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n   286→        model=step.model or config.model,\n   287→        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n   288→        timeout_seconds=step.timeout_seconds,\n   289→        output_format=\"text\",\n   290→        extra_args=extra_args,\n   291→    )\n   292→\n   293→    proc.start()\n   294→\n   295→    # Poll for cancellation while waiting\n   296→    while proc._process is not None and proc._process.poll() is None:\n   297→        if cancel_check():\n   298→            proc.terminate()\n   299→            return StepResult(\n   300→                step=step,\n   301→                status=StepStatus.CANCELLED,\n   302→                attempt=1,\n   303→                gate_failure_reason=\"Cancelled by external signal\",\n   304→                started_at=started_at,\n   305→                finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n   306→            )\n   307→        time.sleep(1)\n   308→\n   309→    exit_code = proc.wait()\n   310→    finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   311→\n   312→    if exit_code == 124:\n   313→        return StepResult(\n   314→            step=step,\n   315→            status=StepStatus.TIMEOUT,\n   316→            attempt=1,\n   317→            gate_failure_reason=f\"Step '{step.id}' timed out after {step.timeout_seconds}s\",\n   318→            started_at=started_at,\n   319→            finished_at=finished_at,\n   320→        )\n   321→\n   322→    if exit_code != 0:\n   323→        return StepResult(\n   324→            step=step,\n   325→            status=StepStatus.FAIL,\n   326→            attempt=1,\n   327→            gate_failure_reason=f\"Step '{step.id}' exited with code {exit_code}\",\n   328→            started_at=started_at,\n   329→            finished_at=finished_at,\n   330→        )\n   331→\n   332→    # Process completed successfully; gate check happens in execute_pipeline\n   347→    return StepResult(\n   348→        step=step,\n   349→        status=StepStatus.PASS,\n   350→        attempt=1,\n   351→        started_at=started_at,\n   352→        finished_at=finished_at,\n   353→    )\n   354→\n   355→\n   356→def build_certify_step(\n   357→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n   358→    findings: list | None = None,\n   359→    context_sections: dict[str, str] | None = None,\n   360→) -> Step:\n   361→    \"\"\"Build a certify Step for execution via execute_pipeline().\n   362→\n   363→    The certify step runs as a standard Step (not ClaudeProcess directly)\n   364→    per spec section 2.5.        gate=SCORE_GATE,\n   492→            timeout_seconds=300,\n   493→            inputs=[debate_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b],\n   494→            retry_limit=1,\n   495→        ),\n   496→        # Step 6: Merge\n   497→        Step(\n   498→            id=\"merge\",\n   499→            prompt=build_merge_prompt(score_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, debate_file),\n   500→            output_file=merge_file,\n   501→            gate=MERGE_GATE,\n   502→            timeout_seconds=600,\n   503→            inputs=[score_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, debate_file],\n   504→            retry_limit=1,\n   505→        ),\n   506→        # Step 7: Test Strategy\n   507→        Step(\n   508→            id=\"test-strategy\",\n   509→            prompt=build_test_strategy_prompt(merge_file, extraction),\n   510→            output_file=test_strat,\n   511→            gate=TEST_STRATEGY_GATE,\n   512→            timeout_seconds=300,\n   513→            inputs=[merge_file, extraction],\n   514→            retry_limit=1,\n   515→        ),\n   516→        # Step 8: Spec Fidelity (after test-strategy, FR-008 through FR-010)\n   517→        Step(\n   518→            id=\"spec-fidelity\",\n   519→            prompt=build_spec_fidelity_prompt(config.spec_file, merge_file),\n   520→            output_file=spec_fidelity_file,\n   521→            gate=None if config.convergence_enabled else SPEC_FIDELITY_GATE,\n   522→            timeout_seconds=600,\n   523→            inputs=[config.spec_file, merge_file],\n   524→            retry_limit=1,\n   525→        ),\n   526→        # Step 9: Wiring Verification (section 5.7, shadow mode trailing gate)\n   527→        Step(\n   528→            id=\"wiring-verification\",\n   529→            prompt=build_wiring_verification_prompt(\n   530→                merge_file, config.spec_file.name\n   531→            ),\n   532→            output_file=out / \"wiring-verification.md\",\n   533→            gate=WIRING_GATE,\n   534→            timeout_seconds=60,\n   535→            inputs=[merge_file, spec_fidelity_file],\n   536→            retry_limit=0,\n   537→            gate_mode=GateMode.TRAILING,\n   538→        ),\n   539→    ]\n   540→\n   541→    return steps\n   542→\n   543→\n   544→def _format_halt_output(results: list[StepResult], config: RoadmapConfig) -> str:\n   545→    \"\"\"Format HALT diagnostic output per spec section 6.2.\"\"\"\n   546→    failed = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n   547→    passed = [r for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.PASS]\n   548→    cancelled = [r for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.CANCELLED]\n   549→\n   550→    if not failed:\n   551→        return \"\"\n   552→\n   553→    fail = failed[-1]\n   554→    step = fail.step\n   555→\n   556→    # Calculate file details if output exists\n   557→    file_info = \"\"\n   558→    if step and step.output_file.exists():\n   559→        content = step.output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   560→        byte_count = len(content.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n   561→        line_count = len(content.splitlines())\n   562→        file_info = f\"  Output size: {byte_count} bytes ({line_count} lines)\\n\"\n   563→    elif step:\n   564→        file_info = \"  Output file: not created\\n\"\n   565→\n   566→    elapsed = f\"{fail.duration_seconds:.0f}s\"\n   567→\n   568→    lines = [\n   569→        f\"ERROR: Roadmap pipeline halted at step '{step.id}' (attempt {fail.attempt}/2)\",\n   570→        f\"  Gate failure: {fail.gate_failure_reason}\",\n   571→        f\"  Output file: {step.output_file}\",\n   572→        file_info.rstrip(),\n   573→        f\"  Step timeout: {step.timeout_seconds}s | Elapsed: {elapsed}\",\n   574→        \"\",\n   575→        f\"Completed steps: {', '.join(f'{r.step.id} (PASS, attempt {r.attempt})' for r in passed) or 'none'}\",\n   576→        f\"Failed step:     {step.id} ({fail.status.value}, attempt {fail.attempt})\",\n   577→    ]\n   578→\n   579→    # Collect skipped steps\n   580→    all_step_ids = _get_all_step_ids(config)\n   581→    executed_ids = {r.step.id for r in results}\n   582→    skipped_ids = [sid for sid in all_step_ids if sid not in executed_ids]\n   583→    if cancelled:\n   584→        skipped_ids = [r.step.id for r in cancelled] + skipped_ids\n   585→\n   586→    if skipped_ids:\n   587→        lines.append(f\"Skipped steps:   {', '.join(skipped_ids)}\")\n   588→\n   589→    lines.extend(\n   590→        [\n   591→            \"\",\n   592→            \"To retry from this step:\",\n   593→            f\"  superclaude roadmap run {config.spec_file} --resume\",\n   594→            \"\",\n   595→            \"To inspect the failing output:\",\n   596→            f\"  cat {step.output_file}\",\n   597→        ]\n   598→    )\n   599→\n   600→    return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n   601→\n   602→\n   603→def _get_all_step_ids(config: RoadmapConfig) -> list[str]:\n   604→    \"\"\"Get all step IDs in pipeline order.\"\"\"\n   605→    agent_a = config.agents[0]\n   606→    agent_b = config.agents[1] if len(config.agents) > 1 else config.agents[0]\n   607→    return [\n   608→        \"extract\",\n   609→        f\"generate-{agent_a.id}\",\n   610→        f\"generate-{agent_b.id}\",\n   611→        \"diff\",\n   612→        \"debate\",\n   613→        \"score\",\n   614→        \"merge\",\n   615→        \"test-strategy\",\n   616→        \"spec-fidelity\",\n   617→        \"wiring-verification\",\n   618→        \"remediate\",\n   619→        \"certify\",\n   620→    ]\n   621→\n   622→\n   623→def _print_terminal_halt(\n   624→    output_dir: Path,\n   625→    remaining_findings: list,\n   626→    attempt_count: int,\n   627→    spec_patch_budget_exhausted: bool = False,\n   628→    file=None,\n   629→) -> None:\n   630→    \"\"\"Print terminal halt message to stderr with finding details (SC-6).\n   631→\n   632→    Outputs:\n   633→    - Attempt count and remaining failing finding count\n   634→    - Per-finding details (ID, severity, description)\n   635→    - Manual-fix instructions including certification report path and resume command\n   636→\n   637→    Args:\n   638→        output_dir: Pipeline output directory (for certification report path)\n   639→        remaining_findings: List of Finding objects that failed remediation\n   640→        attempt_count: Number of remediation attempts made\n   641→        spec_patch_budget_exhausted: If True, note that spec-patch budget is also exhausted\n   642→        file: Output stream (defaults to sys.stderr)\n   643→    \"\"\"\n   644→    if file is None:\n   645→        file = sys.stderr\n   646→\n   647→    certify_path = output_dir / \"certify.md\"\n   648→\n   649→    lines = [\n   650→        \"\",\n   651→        \"=\" * 72,\n   652→        \"TERMINAL HALT: Remediation budget exhausted\",\n   653→        \"=\" * 72,\n   654→        f\"Attempt count: {attempt_count}\",\n   655→        f\"Remaining failing findings: {len(remaining_findings)}\",\n   656→        \"\",\n   657→    ]\n   658→\n   659→    if remaining_findings:\n   660→        lines.append(\"Failing findings:\")\n   661→        for finding in remaining_findings:\n   662→            severity = getattr(finding, \"severity\", \"UNKNOWN\")\n   663→            description = getattr(finding, \"description\", \"\")\n   664→            finding_id = getattr(finding, \"id\", \"UNKNOWN\")\n   665→            lines.append(f\"  - {finding_id} [{severity}]: {description}\")\n   666→        lines.append(\"\")\n   667→\n   668→    lines.extend(\n   669→        [\n   670→            \"Manual fix required:\",\n   671→            f\"  1. Review certification report: {certify_path}\",\n   672→            \"  2. Fix remaining findings manually\",\n   673→            \"  3. Resume pipeline with:\",\n   674→            f\"     superclaude roadmap run --resume\",\n   675→            \"\",\n   676→        ]\n   677→    )\n   678→\n   679→    if spec_patch_budget_exhausted:\n   680→        lines.extend(\n   681→            [\n   682→                \"Note: Both the remediation budget and the spec-patch cycle budget\",\n   683→                \"are exhausted. Full dual-budget recovery is deferred to v2.26.\",\n   684→                \"# TODO(v2.26): implement dual-budget-exhaustion recovery mechanism\",\n   685→                \"\",\n   686→            ]\n   687→        )\n   688→\n   689→    lines.append(\"=\" * 72)\n   690→\n   691→    print(\"\\n\".join(lines), file=file, flush=True)\n   692→\n   693→\n   694→def _check_annotate_deviations_freshness(\n   695→    output_dir: Path,\n   696→    roadmap_file: Path,\n   697→    gate_pass_state: dict[str, bool] | None = None,\n   698→) -> bool:\n   699→    \"\"\"Check if spec-deviations.md hash matches current roadmap.md (SC-8).\n   700→\n   701→    Reads roadmap_hash from spec-deviations.md frontmatter and compares\n   702→    against sha256(roadmap.md). Fail-closed for all error conditions.\n   703→\n   704→    On hash mismatch, resets spec-fidelity and deviation-analysis gate\n   705→    pass state to force re-run (FR-084).\n   706→\n   707→    Returns:\n   708→        True if hash matches (fresh), False for any failure or mismatch.\n   709→    \"\"\"\n   710→    spec_deviations = output_dir / \"spec-deviations.md\"\n   711→\n   712→    # Case 1: spec-deviations.md not found\n   713→    if not spec_deviations.exists():\n   714→        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: spec-deviations.md not found\")\n   715→        return False\n   716→\n   717→    # Case 2: Read error\n   718→    try:\n   719→        content = spec_deviations.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   720→    except OSError as e:\n   721→        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: read error for spec-deviations.md: %s\", e)\n   722→        return False\n   723→\n   724→    # Case 3: Empty file\n   725→    if not content.strip():\n   726→        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: spec-deviations.md is empty\")\n   727→        return False\n   728→\n   729→    # Case 4: Corrupt/missing frontmatter\n   730→    stripped = content.lstrip()\n   731→    if not stripped.startswith(\"---\"):\n   732→        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: spec-deviations.md has no frontmatter\")\n   733→        return False\n   734→\n   735→    rest = stripped[3:].lstrip(\"\\n\")\n   736→    end_idx = rest.find(\"\\n---\")\n   737→    if end_idx == -1:\n   738→        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: spec-deviations.md has corrupt frontmatter\")\n   739→        return False\n   740→\n   741→    # Parse roadmap_hash from frontmatter\n   742→    saved_hash: str | None = None\n   743→    for line in rest[:end_idx].splitlines():\n   744→        line = line.strip()\n   745→        if line.startswith(\"roadmap_hash:\"):\n   746→            saved_hash = line.split(\":\", 1)[1].strip()\n   747→            break\n   748→\n   749→    # Case 5: roadmap_hash field missing or empty\n   750→    if not saved_hash:\n   751→        _log.warning(\n   752→            \"Freshness check: roadmap_hash field missing or empty in spec-deviations.md\"\n   753→        )\n   754→        return False\n   755→\n   756→    # Case 6: roadmap.md not found\n   757→    if not roadmap_file.exists():\n   758→        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: roadmap.md not found at %s\", roadmap_file)\n   759→        return False\n   760→\n   761→    # Case 7: roadmap.md read error\n   762→    try:\n   763→        roadmap_content = roadmap_file.read_bytes()\n   764→    except OSError as e:\n   765→        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: read error for roadmap.md: %s\", e)\n   766→        return False\n   767→\n   768→    # Case 8: Hash comparison\n   769→    current_hash = hashlib.sha256(roadmap_content).hexdigest()\n   770→    if saved_hash != current_hash:\n   771→        _log.warning(\n   772→            \"Freshness check: hash mismatch -- saved=%s current=%s\",\n   773→            saved_hash[:12],\n   774→            current_hash[:12],\n   775→        )\n   776→        # FR-084: reset gate pass state for downstream steps\n   777→        if gate_pass_state is not None:\n   778→            for key in (\"spec-fidelity\", \"deviation-analysis\"):\n   779→                if key in gate_pass_state:\n   780→                    gate_pass_state[key] = False\n   781→        return False\n   782→\n   783→    return True\n   784→\n   785→\n   786→def _check_remediation_budget(\n   787→    output_dir: Path,\n   788→    max_attempts: int = 2,\n   789→    halt_fn: \"Callable[[Path, list, int], None] | None\" = None,\n   790→) -> bool:\n   791→    \"\"\"Check if remediation budget allows another attempt (SC-6).\n   792→\n   793→    Reads remediation_attempts from .roadmap-state.json.\n   794→    If attempts >= max_attempts, calls halt_fn (or _print_terminal_halt)\n   795→    and returns False.\n   796→\n   797→    Non-integer remediation_attempts is coerced to 0 with a WARNING log.\n   798→\n   799→    Args:\n   800→        output_dir: Pipeline output directory containing .roadmap-state.json\n   801→        max_attempts: Maximum allowed remediation attempts (default 2)\n   802→        halt_fn: Optional callable for terminal halt; defaults to _print_terminal_halt\n   803→\n   804→    Returns:\n   805→        True if budget allows another attempt, False if exhausted.\n   806→    \"\"\"\n   807→    state_file = output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n   808→    state = read_state(state_file)\n   809→\n   810→    attempts = 0\n   811→    if state is not None:\n   812→        raw = state.get(\"remediation_attempts\", 0)\n   813→        try:\n   814→            attempts = int(raw)\n   815→        except (ValueError, TypeError):\n   816→            _log.warning(\"Non-integer remediation_attempts %r coerced to 0\", raw)\n   817→            attempts = 0\n   818→\n   819→    if attempts >= max_attempts:\n   820→        if halt_fn is not None:\n   821→            halt_fn(output_dir, [], attempts + 1)\n   822→        else:\n   823→            _print_terminal_halt(\n   824→                output_dir=output_dir,\n   825→                remaining_findings=[],\n   826→                attempt_count=attempts + 1,\n   827→            )\n   828→        return False\n   829→\n   830→    return True\n   831→\n   832→\n   833→def _print_step_start(step: Step) -> None:\n   834→    \"\"\"Print progress output when a step starts.\"\"\"\n   835→    print(f\"[roadmap] Starting step: {step.id}\", flush=True)\n   836→\n   837→\n   838→def _print_step_complete(step: Step, result: StepResult) -> None:\n   839→    \"\"\"Print progress output when a step completes.\"\"\"\n   840→    duration = f\"{result.duration_seconds:.0f}s\"\n   841→    if result.status == StepStatus.PASS:\n   842→        print(\n   843→            f\"[roadmap] Step {step.id}  PASS (attempt {result.attempt}, {duration})\",\n   844→            flush=True,\n   845→        )\n   846→    else:\n   847→        print(\n   848→            f\"[roadmap] Step {step.id}  {result.status.value} (attempt {result.attempt}, {duration})\",\n   849→            flush=True,\n   850→        )\n   851→        if result.gate_failure_reason:\n   852→            print(f\"           Reason: {result.gate_failure_reason}\", flush=True)\n   853→\n   854→\n   855→def _dry_run_output(steps: list[Step | list[Step]]) -> None:\n   856→    \"\"\"Print step plan and gate criteria for --dry-run.\"\"\"\n   857→    step_num = 0\n   858→    for entry in steps:\n   859→        if isinstance(entry, list):\n   860→            for s in entry:\n   861→                step_num += 1\n   862→                _print_step_plan(step_num, s, parallel=True)\n   863→        else:\n   864→            step_num += 1\n   865→            _print_step_plan(step_num, entry)\n   866→\n   867→\n   868→def _print_step_plan(num: int, step: Step, parallel: bool = False) -> None:\n   869→    \"\"\"Print a single step's plan entry.\"\"\"\n   870→    par_label = \" (parallel)\" if parallel else \"\"\n   871→    print(f\"Step {num}{par_label}: {step.id}\")\n   872→    print(f\"  Output: {step.output_file}\")\n   873→    print(f\"  Timeout: {step.timeout_seconds}s\")\n   874→    if step.model:\n   875→        print(f\"  Model: {step.model}\")\n   876→    if step.gate:\n   877→        print(f\"  Gate tier: {step.gate.enforcement_tier}\")\n   878→        print(f\"  Gate min_lines:
