         print(f\"  Semantic checks: {', '.join(checks)}\")\n   886→    print()\n   887→\n   888→\n   889→def _save_state(\n   890→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n   891→    results: list[StepResult],\n   892→    remediate_metadata: dict | None = None,\n   893→    certify_metadata: dict | None = None,\n   894→) -> None:\n   895→    \"\"\"Write .roadmap-state.json to output_dir via atomic tmp + os.replace().\n   896→\n   897→    Preserves existing ``validation``, ``fidelity_status``, ``remediate``,\n   898→    and ``certify`` keys if present. Accepts optional remediate/certify\n   899→    metadata dicts for Phase 6 state finalization.\n   900→\n   901→    Defense-in-depth guards:\n   902→    - No-progress guard: skip write when no steps passed (prevents corruption\n   903→      from broken resumes that fail immediately).\n   904→    - Agent-mismatch guard: preserve original agents in state when no generate\n   905→      steps ran (prevents overwriting correct agent config with wrong agents).\n   906→    \"\"\"\n   907→    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n   908→\n   909→    # No-progress guard: if the results list is empty (no steps were even\n   910→    # attempted), do not write state. This prevents corruption from broken\n   911→    # resumes that produce zero results. Any non-empty results list indicates\n   912→    # the pipeline made progress and state should be recorded.\n   913→    step_results = [r for r in results if r.step]\n   914→    if not step_results:\n   915→        _log.info(\"State not saved: no step results in this run\")\n   916→        return\n   917→\n   918→    # Preserve existing keys across state rewrites\n   919→    existing = read_state(state_file)\n   920→\n   921→    # Agent-mismatch guard: if agents differ from state and no generate steps\n   922→    # ran, preserve original agents to prevent corruption\n   923→    if existing is not None:\n   924→        saved_agents = existing.get(\"agents\", [])\n   925→        current_agents = [\n   926→            {\"model\": a.model, \"persona\": a.persona} for a in config.agents\n   927→        ]\n   928→        if saved_agents and saved_agents != current_agents:\n   929→            generate_ran = any(\n   930→                r.step\n   931→                and r.step.id.startswith(\"generate-\")\n   932→                and r.status == StepStatus.PASS\n   933→                for r in results\n   934→            )\n   935→            if not generate_ran:\n   936→                _log.warning(\n   937→                    \"Agents differ from state file and no generate steps ran; \"\n   938→                    \"preserving original agents in state\"\n   939→                )\n   940→                return\n   941→\n   942→    existing_validation = existing.get(\"validation\") if existing else None\n   943→    existing_fidelity = existing.get(\"fidelity_status\") if existing else None\n   944→    existing_remediate = existing.get(\"remediate\") if existing else None\n   945→    existing_certify = existing.get(\"certify\") if existing else None\n   946→\n   947→    spec_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n   948→\n   949→    state = {\n   950→        \"schema_version\": 1,\n   951→        \"spec_file\": str(config.spec_file),\n   952→        \"spec_hash\": spec_hash,\n   953→        \"agents\": [{\"model\": a.model, \"persona\": a.persona} for a in config.agents],\n   954→        \"depth\": config.depth,\n   955→        \"last_run\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n   956→        \"steps\": {\n   957→            r.step.id: {\n   958→                \"status\": r.status.value,\n   959→                \"attempt\": r.attempt,\n   960→                \"output_file\": str(r.step.output_file),\n   961→                \"started_at\": r.started_at.isoformat(),\n   962→                \"completed_at\": r.finished_at.isoformat(),\n   963→            }\n   964→            for r in results\n   965→            if r.step\n   966→        },\n   967→    }\n   968→\n   969→    # Merge with existing state (preserve steps not in this run)\n   970→    if existing:\n   971→        existing_steps = existing.get(\"steps\", {})\n   972→        for step_id, step_data in existing_steps.items():\n   973→            if step_id not in state[\"steps\"]:\n   974→                state[\"steps\"][step_id] = step_data\n   975→\n   976→    if existing_validation is not None:\n   977→        state[\"validation\"] = existing_validation\n   978→\n   979→    # Derive fidelity_status from spec-fidelity step result\n   980→    fidelity_result = next(\n   981→        (r for r in results if r.step and r.step.id == \"spec-fidelity\"),\n   982→        None,\n   983→    )\n   984→    if fidelity_result is not None:\n   985→        state[\"fidelity_status\"] = _derive_fidelity_status(fidelity_result)\n   986→    elif existing_fidelity is not None:\n   987→        state[\"fidelity_status\"] = existing_fidelity\n   988→\n   989→    # Remediate metadata (spec §3.1)\n   990→    if remediate_metadata is not None:\n   991→        state[\"remediate\"] = remediate_metadata\n   992→    elif existing_remediate is not None:\n   993→        state[\"remediate\"] = existing_remediate\n   994→\n   995→    # Certify metadata (spec §3.1)\n   996→    if certify_metadata is not None:\n   997→        state[\"certify\"] = certify_metadata\n   998→    elif existing_certify is not None:\n   999→        state[\"certify\"] = existing_certify\n  1000→\n  1001→    write_state(state, state_file)\n  1002→\n  1003→\n  1004→def _derive_fidelity_status(result: StepResult) -> str:\n  1005→    \"\"\"Derive fidelity_status enum from a spec-fidelity StepResult.\n  1006→\n  1007→    Returns one of: 'pass', 'fail', 'skipped', 'degraded'.\n  1008→    \"\"\"\n  1009→    if result.status == StepStatus.PASS:\n  1010→        # Check if the output indicates degraded mode\n  1011→        if result.step and result.step.output_file.exists():\n  1012→            content = result.step.output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n  1013→            if \"validation_complete: false\" in content:\n  1014→                return \"degraded\"\n  1015→        return \"pass\"\n  1016→    if result.status == StepStatus.SKIPPED:\n  1017→        return \"skipped\"\n  1018→    if result.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT):\n  1019→        return \"fail\"\n  1020→    return \"skipped\"\n  1021→\n  1022→\n  1023→def generate_degraded_report(\n  1024→    output_file: Path,\n  1025→    failed_agent: str,\n  1026→    failure_reason: str,\n  1027→) -> None:\n  1028→    \"\"\"Generate a degraded fidelity report when the agent fails.\n  1029→\n  1030→    Produces a report with validation_complete=false and\n  1031→    fidelity_check_attempted=true so the SPEC_FIDELITY_GATE\n  1032→    can distinguish degraded from clean passes (NFR-007).\n  1033→\n  1034→    The degraded report names the failed agent and reason in the body.\n  1035→    \"\"\"\n  1036→    report = (\n  1037→        \"---\\n\"\n  1038→        \"high_severity_count: 0\\n\"\n  1039→        \"medium_severity_count: 0\\n\"\n  1040→        \"low_severity_count: 0\\n\"\n  1041→        \"total_deviations: 0\\n\"\n  1042→        \"validation_complete: false\\n\"\n  1043→        \"fidelity_check_attempted: true\\n\"\n  1044→        \"tasklist_ready: false\\n\"\n  1045→        \"---\\n\"\n  1046→        \"\\n\"\n  1047→        \"## Degraded Fidelity Report\\n\"\n  1048→        \"\\n\"\n  1049→        \"Spec-fidelity validation could not be completed.\\n\"\n  1050→        \"\\n\"\n  1051→        f\"**Failed Agent**: {failed_agent}\\n\"\n  1052→        f\"**Failure Reason**: {failure_reason}\\n\"\n  1053→        \"\\n\"\n  1054→        \"This is a degraded report produced after agent failure and retry \"\n  1055→        \"exhaustion. No deviations were analyzed. The pipeline continues \"\n  1056→        \"in degraded mode with validation_complete=false.\\n\"\n  1057→        \"\\n\"\n  1058→        \"### Recommended Actions\\n\"\n  1059→        \"\\n\"\n  1060→        \"1. Investigate the agent failure\\n\"\n  1061→        \"2. Re-run the spec-fidelity step manually\\n\"\n  1062→        \"3. Review the roadmap against the specification\\n\"\n  1063→    )\n  1064→    output_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n  1065→    output_file.write_text(report, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n  1066→\n  1067→\n  1068→def build_remediate_metadata(\n  1069→    status: str,\n  1070→    scope: str,\n  1071→    findings_total: int,\n  1072→    findings_actionable: int,\n  1073→    findings_fixed: int,\n  1074→    findings_failed: int,\n  1075→    findings_skipped: int,\n  1076→    agents_spawned: int,\n  1077→    tasklist_file: str,\n  1078→) -> dict:\n  1079→    \"\"\"Build remediate metadata dict for state schema §3.1.\n  1080→\n  1081→    Parameters map 1:1 to .roadmap-state.json ``remediate`` entry fields.\n  1082→    \"\"\"\n  1083→    return {\n  1084→        \"status\": status,\n  1085→        \"scope\": scope,\n  1086→        \"findings_total\": findings_total,\n  1087→        \"findings_actionable\": findings_actionable,\n  1088→        \"findings_fixed\": findings_fixed,\n  1089→        \"findings_failed\": findings_failed,\n  1090→        \"findings_skipped\": findings_skipped,\n  1091→        \"agents_spawned\": agents_spawned,\n  1092→        \"tasklist_file\": tasklist_file,\n  1093→        \"timestamp\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n  1094→    }\n  1095→\n  1096→\n  1097→def build_certify_metadata(\n  1098→    status: str,\n  1099→    findings_verified: int,\n  1100→    findings_passed: int,\n  1101→    findings_failed: int,\n  1102→    certified: bool,\n  1103→    report_file: str,\n  1104→) -> dict:\n  1105→    \"\"\"Build certify metadata dict for state schema §3.1.\n  1106→\n  1107→    Parameters map 1:1 to .roadmap-state.json ``certify`` entry fields.\n  1108→    \"\"\"\n  1109→    return {\n  1110→        \"status\": status,\n  1111→        \"findings_verified\": findings_verified,\n  1112→        \"findings_passed\": findings_passed,\n  1113→        \"findings_failed\": findings_failed,\n  1114→        \"certified\": certified,\n  1115→        \"report_file\": report_file,\n  1116→        \"timestamp\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n  1117→    }\n  1118→\n  1119→\n  1120→def derive_pipeline_status(state: dict) -> str:\n  1121→    \"\"\"Derive the overall pipeline status from state transitions.\n  1122→\n  1123→    State transitions at each boundary:\n  1124→    - post-validate: validated | validated-with-issues\n  1125→    - post-remediate: remediated\n  1126→    - post-certify: certified | certified-with-caveats\n  1127→\n  1128→    Returns one of: 'pending', 'validated', 'validated-with-issues',\n  1129→    'remediated', 'certified', 'certified-with-caveats'.\n  1130→    \"\"\"\n  1131→    certify = state.get(\"certify\")\n  1132→    if certify is not None:\n  1133→        if certify.get(\"certified\", False):\n  1134→            return \"certified\"\n  1135→        return \"certified-with-caveats\"\n  1136→\n  1137→    remediate = state.get(\"remediate\")\n  1138→    if remediate is not None:\n  1139→        return \"remediated\"\n  1140→\n  1141→    validation = state.get(\"validation\")\n  1142→    if validation is not None:\n  1143→        if validation.get(\"status\") == \"pass\":\n  1144→            return \"validated\"\n  1145→        if validation.get(\"status\") == \"fail\":\n  1146→            return \"validated-with-issues\"\n  1147→\n  1148→    return \"pending\"\n  1149→\n  1150→\n  1151→def write_state(state: dict, path: Path) -> None:\n  1152→    \"\"\"Write state dict to path atomically via tmp file + os.replace().\"\"\"\n  1153→    import os as _os\n  1154→\n  1155→    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n  1156→    tmp = path.with_suffix(\".tmp\")\n  1157→    tmp.write_text(json.dumps(state, indent=2), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n  1158→    _os.replace(str(tmp), str(path))\n  1159→\n  1160→\n  1161→def read_state(path: Path) -> dict | None:\n  1162→    \"\"\"Read state from path with graceful recovery on missing/malformed files.\"\"\"\n  1163→    if not path.exists():\n  1164→        return None\n  1165→    try:\n  1166→        text = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n  1167→        if not text.strip():\n  1168→            return None\n  1169→        return json.loads(text)\n  1170→    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n  1171→        return None\n  1172→\n  1173→\n  1174→def apply_decomposition_pass(deliverables: list[Deliverable]) -> list[Deliverable]:\n  1175→    \"\"\"Post-generation decomposition pass for the roadmap pipeline.\n  1176→\n  1177→    Runs after deliverable generation, before output formatting.\n  1178→    Splits behavioral deliverables into Implement/Verify pairs.\n  1179→\n  1180→    Idempotent: running twice produces identical results because\n  1181→    already-decomposed deliverables (IDs ending .a/.b) are skipped.\n  1182→\n  1183→    Preserves milestone-internal ordering: deliverables within each\n  1184→    milestone maintain their relative order after decomposition.\n  1185→    \"\"\"\n  1186→    return decompose_deliverables(deliverables)\n  1187→\n  1188→\n  1189→def _restore_from_state(\n  1190→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n  1191→    agents_explicit: bool,\n  1192→    depth_explicit: bool,\n  1193→) -> RoadmapConfig:\n  1194→    \"\"\"Restore agent/depth specs from .roadmap-state.json when not explicit.\n  1195→\n  1196→    On --resume, if the user did not explicitly pass --agents or --depth,\n  1197→    restore the values from the state file so the pipeline uses the same\n  1198→    configuration as the original run.\n  1199→    \"\"\"\n  1200→    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n  1201→    state = read_state(state_file)\n  1202→    if state is None:\n  1203→        if not agents_explicit or not depth_explicit:\n  1204→            print(\n  1205→                \"WARNING: --resume with no state file found. \"\n  1206→                \"Using defaults for unspecified options.\",\n  1207→                file=sys.stderr,\n  1208→                flush=True,\n  1209→            )\n  1210→        _log.info(\"No state file found; using CLI agents for fresh run\")\n  1211→        return config\n  1212→\n  1213→    # Restore agents from state if user did not explicitly pass --agents\n  1214→    if not agents_explicit:\n  1215→        saved_agents = state.get(\"agents\")\n  1216→        if saved_agents and isinstance(saved_agents, list):\n  1217→            try:\n  1218→                restored = [\n  1219→                    AgentSpec(model=a[\"model\"], persona=a[\"persona\"])\n  1220→                    for a in saved_agents\n  1221→                ]\n  1222→            except (KeyError, TypeError) as exc:\n  1223→                _log.warning(\n  1224→                    \"Malformed agents in state file (%s); using CLI agents\", exc\n  1225→                )\n  1226→                return config\n  1227→\n  1228→            if restored != config.agents:\n  1229→                agent_str = \", \".join(f\"{a.model}:{a.persona}\" for a in restored)\n  1230→                print(\n  1231→                    f\"[roadmap] Restoring agents from state file: {agent_str}\",\n  1232→                    flush=True,\n  1233→                )\n  1234→            config.agents = restored\n  1235→        else:\n  1236→            _log.warning(\"State file has no agents key; using CLI agents\")\n  1237→\n  1238→    # Restore depth from state if user did not explicitly pass --depth\n  1239→    if not depth_explicit:\n  1240→        saved_depth = state.get(\"depth\")\n  1241→        if saved_depth:\n  1242→            if saved_depth != config.depth:\n  1243→                print(\n  1244→                    f\"WARNING: --depth mismatch.\\n\"\n  1245→                    f\"  State file depth: {saved_depth}\\n\"\n  1246→                    f\"  Current depth:    {config.depth}\\n\"\n  1247→                    f\"  Using state file depth: {saved_depth}\\n\",\n  1248→                    file=sys.stderr,\n  1249→                    flush=True,\n  1250→                )\n  1251→            config.depth = saved_depth\n  1252→\n  1253→    return config\n  1254→\n  1255→\n  1256→def execute_roadmap(\n  1257→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n  1258→    resume: bool = False,\n  1259→    no_validate: bool = False,\n  1260→    auto_accept: bool = False,\n  1261→    agents_explicit: bool = True,\n  1262→    depth_explicit: bool = True,\n  1263→) -> None:\n  1264→    \"\"\"Execute the roadmap generation pipeline.\n  1265→\n  1266→    Builds the step list, handles --dry-run and --resume, then\n  1267→    delegates to execute_pipeline() with roadmap_run_step.\n  1268→\n  1269→    After all 9 steps pass, auto-invokes validation unless\n  1270→    --no-validate is set or --resume halted on a failed step.\n  1271→\n  1272→    Note: --no-validate does NOT skip the spec-fidelity step\n  1273→    (FR-010, AC-005). It only skips the post-pipeline validation\n  1274→    subsystem.\n  1275→\n  1276→    Args:\n  1277→        auto_accept: When True, the spec-patch resume cycle skips the\n  1278→            interactive prompt and proceeds automatically if evidence is\n  1279→            found. When False (default), the cycle prompts the user.\n  1280→            This is an internal parameter, not exposed on the CLI.\n  1281→        agents_explicit: Whether --agents was explicitly passed on the CLI.\n  1282→        depth_explicit: Whether --depth was explicitly passed on the CLI.\n  1283→    \"\"\"\n  1284→    config.output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n  1285→\n  1286→    # On --resume with defaulted agents/depth, restore from state file\n  1287→    if resume:\n  1288→        config = _restore_from_state(\n  1289→            config,\n  1290→            agents_explicit=agents_explicit,\n  1291→            depth_explicit=depth_explicit,\n  1292→        )\n  1293→\n  1294→    # Apply hardcoded defaults for any still-None fields (non-resume or no state)\n  1295→    if not config.agents:\n  1296→        config.agents = [AgentSpec(\"opus\", \"architect\"), AgentSpec(\"haiku\", \"architect\")]\n  1297→    if not config.depth:\n  1298→        config.depth = \"standard\"\n  1299→\n  1300→    # FR-2.24.1.11: Recursion guard — local variable, per-invocation\n  1301→    _spec_patch_cycle_count = 0\n  1302→\n  1303→    # FR-2.24.1.9 Condition 3: Capture spec hash at function entry\n  1304→    initial_spec_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n  1305→\n  1306→    steps = _build_steps(config)\n  1307→\n  1308→    # --dry-run: print plan and exit\n  1309→    if config.dry_run:\n  1310→        _dry_run_output(steps)\n  1311→        return\n  1312→\n  1313→    # --resume: check which steps already pass their gates\n  1314→    if resume:\n  1315→        from ..pipeline.gates import gate_passed\n  1316→\n  1317→        steps = _apply_resume(steps, config, gate_passed)\n  1318→\n  1319→    # Execute pipeline\n  1320→    results = execute_pipeline(\n  1321→        steps=steps,\n  1322→        config=config,\n  1323→        run_step=roadmap_run_step,\n  1324→        on_step_start=_print_step_start,\n  1325→        on_step_complete=_print_step_complete,\n  1326→    )\n  1327→\n  1328→    # Save state\n  1329→    _save_state(config, results)\n  1330→\n  1331→    # Check for failures\n  1332→    failures = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n  1333→    if failures:\n  1334→        # FR-2.24.1.9: Check if spec-fidelity failed and auto-resume is possible\n  1335→        spec_fidelity_failed = any(\n  1336→            r.step\n  1337→            and r.step.id == \"spec-fidelity\"\n  1338→            and r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)\n  1339→            for r in results\n  1340→        )\n  1341→\n  1342→        if spec_fidelity_failed:\n  1343→            resumed = _apply_resume_after_spec_patch(\n  1344→                config=config,\n  1345→                results=results,\n  1346→                auto_accept=auto_accept,\n  1347→                initial_spec_hash=initial_spec_hash,\n  1348→                cycle_count=_spec_patch_cycle_count,\n  1349→            )\n  1350→            if resumed:\n  1351→                _spec_patch_cycle_count += 1\n  1352→                # Cycle complete — resumed pipeline ran to completion or failure\n  1353→                # inside _apply_resume_after_spec_patch. If it returned True,\n  1354→                # we're done (either success or failure was handled internally).\n  1355→                return\n  1356→\n  1357→        halt_msg = _format_halt_output(results, config)\n  1358→        print(halt_msg, file=sys.stderr)\n  1359→        sys.exit(1)\n  1360→\n  1361→    print(f\"\\n[roadmap] Pipeline complete: {len(results)} steps passed\", flush=True)\n  1362→\n  1363→    # Auto-invoke validation after successful pipeline completion\n  1364→    if no_validate:\n  1365→        print(\"[roadmap] Validation skipped (--no-validate)\", flush=True)\n  1366→        _save_validation_status(config, \"skipped\")\n  1367→        return\n  1368→\n  1369→    # Check if validation already completed (--resume path)\n  1370→    if resume:\n  1371→        state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n  1372→        state = read_state(state_file)\n  1373→        if state and \"validation\" in state:\n  1374→            saved = state[\"validation\"]\n  1375→            if saved.get(\"status\") in (\"pass\", \"fail\"):\n  1376→                print(\n  1377→                    f\"[roadmap] Validation already completed ({saved['status']}), skipping\",\n  1378→                    flush=True,\n  1379→                )\n  1380→                return\n  1381→\n  1382→    _auto_invoke_validate(config)\n  1383→\n  1384→\n  1385→def _find_qualifying_deviation_files(\n  1386→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n  1387→    results: list[StepResult],\n  1388→) -> list:\n  1389→    \"\"\"Find deviation files written after spec-fidelity started.\n  1390→\n  1391→    Returns qualifying DeviationRecord objects, or empty list if\n  1392→    conditions are not met.\n  1393→\n  1394→    Implementation detail — not specified in the spec. Extracted for\n  1395→    testability of the three-condition detection gate.\n  1396→    \"\"\"\n  1397→    from .spec_patch import scan_accepted_deviation_records\n  1398→\n  1399→    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n  1400→    state = read_state(state_file)\n  1401→    if state is None:\n  1402→        return []\n  1403→\n  1404→    # Get spec-fidelity started_at timestamp\n  1405→    steps_state = state.get(\"steps\", {})\n  1406→    fidelity_state = steps_state.get(\"spec-fidelity\", {})\n  1407→    started_at_str = fidelity_state.get(\"started_at\")\n  1408→\n  1409→    # Fail-closed: if started_at is absent, condition not met\n  1410→    if not started_at_str:\n  1411→        return []\n  1412→\n  1413→    try:\n  1414→        started_at_ts = datetime.fromisoformat(started_at_str).timestamp()\n  1415→    except (ValueError, TypeError):\n  1416→        return []\n  1417→\n  1418→    # Scan all deviation records\n  1419→    all_records = scan_accepted_deviation_records(config.output_dir)\n  1420→    if not all_records:\n  1421→        return []\n  1422→\n  1423→    # Filter to records written AFTER spec-fidelity started.\n  1424→    # Strict > (not >=): a record with mtime == started_at was written before\n  1425→    # or exactly at step start, so it cannot be evidence of a subprocess fix.\n  1426→    # Caveat: HFS+ and some NFS mounts have 1-second mtime resolution, so\n  1427→    # records created within the same second as started_at may be missed.\n  1428→    qualifying = [r for r in all_records if r.mtime > started_at_ts]\n  1429→    return qualifying\n  1430→\n  1431→\n  1432→def _apply_resume_after_spec_patch(\n  1433→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n  1434→    results: list[StepResult],\n  1435→    auto_accept: bool,\n  1436→    initial_spec_hash: str,\n  1437→    cycle_count: int,\n  1438→) -> bool:\n  1439→    \"\"\"Attempt a single spec-patch auto-resume cycle after spec-fidelity FAIL.\n  1440→\n  1441→    Evaluates the three-condition detection gate (FR-2.24.1.9):\n  1442→      1. Recursion guard: cycle_count == 0\n  1443→      2. Qualifying deviation files exist with mtime > started_at\n  1444→      3. Spec file hash changed since run started (initial_spec_hash)\n  1445→\n  1446→    If all conditions pass, executes the six-step disk-reread sequence\n  1447→    (FR-2.24.1.10) and re-runs the pipeline via _apply_resume.\n  1448→\n  1449→    Single-writer assumption: no concurrent writer modifies\n  1450→    .roadmap-state.json between the reread and write steps.\n  1451→\n  1452→    Returns True if the cycle was attempted (regardless of outcome),\n  1453→    False if conditions were not met.\n  1454→    \"\"\"\n  1455→    # FR-2.24.1.11: Recursion guard\n  1456→    if cycle_count >= 1:\n  1457→        print(\n  1458→            \"[roadmap] Spec-patch cycle already exhausted \"\n  1459→            f\"(cycle_count={cycle_count}). Proceeding to normal failure.\",\n  1460→            flush=True,\n  1461→        )\n  1462→        return False\n  1463→\n  1464→    # FR-2.24.1.9 Condition 2: Qualifying deviation files\n  1465→    qualifying = _find_qualifying_deviation_files(config, results)\n  1466→    if not qualifying:\n  1467→        return False\n  1468→\n  1469→    # FR-2.24.1.9 Condition 3: Spec hash changed since run started\n  1470→    current_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n  1471→    if current_hash == initial_spec_hash:\n  1472→        return False\n  1473→\n  1474→    # All three conditions met — enter the cycle\n  1475→    # FR-2.24.1.12: Cycle entry logging\n  1476→    print(\n  1477→        f\"[roadmap] Spec patched by subprocess. \"\n  1478→        f\"Found {len(qualifying)} accepted deviation record(s).\",\n  1479→        flush=True,\n  1480→    )\n  1481→    print(\n  1482→        \"[roadmap] Triggering spec-hash sync and resume (cycle 1/1).\",\n  1483→        flush=True,\n  1484→    )\n  1485→\n  1486→    # FR-2.24.1.10: Six-step disk-reread sequence\n  1487→    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n  1488→\n  1489→    # Step 1: Re-read state from disk\n  1490→    _fresh_state = read_state(state_file)  # noqa: F841\n  1491→\n  1492→    # Step 2: Recompute spec hash\n  1493→    new_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n  1494→\n  1495→    # Step 3: Atomic write of new hash\n  1496→    try:\n  1497→        # Read again for fresh copy, update only spec_hash, write atomically\n  1498→        fresh_for_write = read_state(state_file) or {}\n  1499→        fresh_for_write[\"spec_hash\"] = new_hash\n  1500→        write_state(fresh_for_write, state_file)\n  1501→    except OSError as exc:\n  1502→        print(\n  1503→            f\"[roadmap] ERROR: Failed to update spec_hash during \"\n  1504→            f\"auto-resume cycle: {exc}. Falling through to normal failure.\",\n  1505→            file=sys.stderr,\n  1506→            flush=True,\n  1507→        )\n  1508→        return True  # Cycle was attempted but failed — don't retry\n  1509→\n  1510→    # Step 4: Re-read state from disk AGAIN (this is what _apply_resume gets)\n  1511→    post_write_state = read_state(state_file)\n  1512→    if post_write_state is None:\n  1513→        print(\n  1514→            \"[roadmap] ERROR: Could not re-read state after write. \"\n  1515→            \"Falling through to normal failure.\",\n  1516→            file=sys.stderr,\n  1517→            flush=True,\n  1518→        )\n  1519→        return True\n  1520→\n  1521→    # Step 5: Rebuild steps\n  1522→    steps = _build_steps(config)\n  1523→\n  1524→    # Step 6: Apply resume with post-write state\n  1525→    from ..pipeline.gates import gate_passed\n  1526→\n  1527→    steps = _apply_resume(steps, config, gate_passed)\n  1528→\n  1529→    # Re-execute pipeline from the resumed point\n  1530→    resumed_results = execute_pipeline(\n  1531→        steps=steps,\n  1532→        config=config,\n  1533→        run_step=roadmap_run_step,\n  1534→        on_step_start=_print_step_start,\n  1535→        on_step_complete=_print_step_complete,\n  1536→    )\n  1537→\n  1538→    # Save state from resumed run\n  1539→    _save_state(config, resumed_results)\n  1540→\n  1541→    # FR-2.24.1.12: Cycle completion logging\n  1542→    print(\"[roadmap] Spec-patch resume cycle complete.\", flush=True)\n  1543→\n  1544→    # Check if resumed pipeline also failed\n  1545→    resumed_failures = [\n  1546→        r for r in resumed_results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)\n  1547→    ]\n  1548→    if resumed_failures:\n  1549→        # FR-2.24.1.13: Normal failure on cycle exhaustion\n  1550→        # Use second-run results only\n  1551→        halt_msg = _format_halt_output(resumed_results, config)\n  1552→        print(halt_msg, file=sys.stderr)\n  1553→        sys.exit(1)\n  1554→\n  1555→    # Any of its input files are in the dirty set, OR\n  1737→    3. \"\"\"Read input files and return their contents as fenced code blocks.\n\n    Each file is wrapped in a fenced block with a ``# <path>`` header.\n    Returns an empty string when *input_paths* is empty (no-op).\n    \"\"\"\n    if not input_paths:\n        return \"\"\n\n    blocks: list[str] = []\n    for p in input_paths:\n        content = Path(p).read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        blocks.append(f\"# {p}\\n```\\n{content}\\n```\")\n    return \"\\n\\n\".join(blocks)\n\n\ndef _sanitize_output(output_file: Path) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Strip conversational preamble and leading blank lines before YAML\n    frontmatter in a step output file.\n\n    Reads the file, strips leading blank lines, finds the first ``^---``\n    line, and removes everything before it.  Uses atomic write (write to\n    ``.tmp`` then ``os.replace()``) to prevent partial file states.\n\n    Returns the byte count of the stripped content (0 when file already\n    starts with ``---`` and has no preamble).\n    \"\"\"\n    import os\n    import re\n\n    try:\n        raw = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        return 0\n\n    # Strip leading blank lines (LLMs sometimes emit \\n\\n before ---)\n    content = raw.lstrip(\"\\n\\r\\t \")\n\n    # Already starts with frontmatter delimiter -- no preamble to strip\n    if content.startswith(\"---\"):\n        if content == raw:\n            return 0\n        # Leading whitespace only -- write back the stripped version\n        preamble_bytes = len(raw.encode(\"utf-8\")) - len(content.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n        tmp_file = output_file.with_suffix(output_file.suffix + \".tmp\")\n        tmp_file.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        os.replace(tmp_file, output_file)\n        _log.info(\n            \"Stripped %d-byte leading whitespace from %s\",\n            preamble_bytes,\n            output_file,\n        )\n        return preamble_bytes\n\n    # Search for the first ^--- line (conversational preamble case)\n    match = re.search(r\"^---[ \\t]*$\", content, re.MULTILINE)\n    if match is None:\n        # No frontmatter found at all -- leave file unchanged\n        return 0\n\n    preamble = content[: match.start()]\n    cleaned = content[match.start():]\n    # Total bytes stripped = leading whitespace + conversational preamble\n    preamble_bytes = len(raw.encode(\"utf-8\")) - len(cleaned.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n\n    # Atomic write: tmp file + os.replace\n    tmp_file = output_file.with_suffix(output_file.suffix + \".tmp\")\n    tmp_file.write_text(cleaned, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    os.replace(tmp_file, output_file)\n\n    _log.info(\"Stripped %d-byte preamble from %s\", preamble_bytes, output_file)\n    return preamble_bytes\n\n\ndef _inject_pipeline_diagnostics(\n    output_file: Path,\n    started_at: datetime,\n    finished_at: datetime,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Inject executor-populated pipeline_diagnostics into extraction frontmatter.\n\n    The LLM cannot reliably produce execution timing or environment metadata,\n    so the executor injects these fields post-subprocess (FR-033).\n    \"\"\"\n    content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").lstrip(\"\\n\\r\\t \")\n    if not content.startswith(\"---\"):\n        return\n\n    # Find end of frontmatter\n    end_idx = content.find(\"\\n---\", 3)\n    if end_idx == -1:\n        return\n\n    # Idempotency: skip if already injected\n    frontmatter = content[3:end_idx]\n    if \"pipeline_diagnostics:\" in frontmatter:\n        return\n\n    elapsed = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n    diagnostics_line = (\n        f\"pipeline_diagnostics: \"\n        f\"{{elapsed_seconds: {elapsed:.1f}, \"\n        f'started_at: \"{started_at.isoformat()}\", '\n        f'finished_at: \"{finished_at.isoformat()}\"}}'\n    )\n\n    # Insert before the closing ---\n    new_content = content[:end_idx] + \"\\n\" + diagnostics_line + content[end_idx:]\n    output_file.write_text(new_content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n\ndef _inject_provenance_fields(\n    output_file: Path,\n    spec_source: str,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Inject provenance fields into test-strategy frontmatter.\n\n    The test-strategy step's LLM output may omit provenance metadata\n    (spec_source, generated, generator) since the prompt focuses on\n    test strategy content. The executor injects these fields post-subprocess\n    to satisfy TEST_STRATEGY_GATE required_frontmatter_fields.\n    \"\"\"\n    content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").lstrip(\"\\n\\r\\t \")\n    if not content.startswith(\"---\"):\n        return\n\n    # Wiring-verification: run static analysis directly, no Claude subprocess.\n    # Returns PASS unconditionally; gate evaluation is handled separately by\n    # the trailing gate runner (section 5.7.1).\n    if step.id == \"wiring-verification\":\n        from ..audit.wiring_gate import run_wiring_analysis, emit_report\n        from ..audit.wiring_config import WiringConfig\n\n        wiring_config = WiringConfig(rollout_mode=\"soft\")\n        source_dir = config.output_dir.parent if hasattr(config, 'output_dir') else Path(\".\")\n        report = run_wiring_analysis(wiring_config, source_dir)\n        step.output_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        emit_report(report, step.output_file)\n        return StepResult(\n            step=step,\n            status=StepStatus.PASS,\n            attempt=1,\n            started_at=started_at,\n            finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n        )\n\n    # Inline embedding: read input files into the prompt instead of --file flags\n    # --file is broken (cloud download mechanism, not local file injector) so\n    # inline embedding is always used regardless of composed prompt size.\n    embedded = _embed_inputs(step.inputs)\n    if embedded:\n        composed = step.prompt + \"\\n\\n\" + embedded\n        if len(composed.encode(\"utf-8\")) > _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT:\n            # <= is intentional; _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT = 120 KB is safely below MAX_ARG_STRLEN = 128 KB\n            _log.warning(\n                \"Step '%s': composed prompt exceeds %d bytes; embedding inline anyway\"\n                \" (--file fallback is unavailable)\",\n                step.id,\n                _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT,\n            )\n        effective_prompt = composed\n        extra_args: list[str] = []\n    else:\n        effective_prompt = step.prompt\n        extra_args = []\n\n    proc = ClaudeProcess(\n        prompt=effective_prompt,\n        output_file=step.output_file,\n        error_file=step.output_file.with_suffix(\".err\"),\n        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n        model=step.model or config.model,\n        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n        timeout_seconds=step.timeout_seconds,\n        output_format=\"text\",\n        extra_args=extra_args,\n    )\n\n    proc.start()\n\n    # Poll for cancellation while waiting\n    while proc._process is not None and proc._process.poll() is None:\n        if cancel_check():\n            proc.terminate()\n            return StepResult(\n                step=step,\n                status=StepStatus.CANCELLED,\n                attempt=1,\n                gate_failure_reason=\"Cancelled by external signal\",\n                started_at=started_at,\n                finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n            )\n        time.sleep(1)\n\n    exit_code = proc.wait()\n    finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return StepResult(\n            step=step,\n            status=StepStatus.TIMEOUT,\n            attempt=1,\n            gate_failure_reason=f\"Step '{step.id}' timed out after {step.timeout_seconds}s\",\n            started_at=started_at,\n            finished_at=finished_at,\n        )\n\n    if exit_code != 0:\n        return StepResult(\n            step=step,\n            status=StepStatus.FAIL,\n            attempt=1,\n            gate_failure_reason=f\"Step '{step.id}' exited with code {exit_code}\",\n            started_at=started_at,\n            finished_at=finished_at,\n        )\n\n    # Sanitize output: strip conversational preamble before gate validation\n    _sanitize_output(step.output_file)\n\n    # Inject executor-populated fields into extract step frontmatter (FR-033)\n    if step.id == \"extract\" and step.output_file.exists():\n        _inject_pipeline_diagnostics(step.output_file, started_at, finished_at)\n\n    # Inject provenance fields into test-strategy output\n    if step.id == \"test-strategy\" and step.output_file.exists():\n        spec_source = (\n            config.spec_file.name if hasattr(config, \"spec_file\") else \"unknown\"\n        )\n        _inject_provenance_fields(step.output_file, spec_source)\n\n    # Process completed successfully; gate check happens in execute_pipeline\n    return StepResult(\n        step=step,\n        status=StepStatus.PASS,\n        attempt=1,\n        started_at=started_at,\n        finished_at=finished_at,\n    )\n\n\ndef build_certify_step(\n    config: RoadmapConfig,\n    findings: list | None = None,\n    context_sections: dict[str, str] | None = None,\n) -> Step:\n    \"\"\"Build a certify Step for execution via execute_pipeline().\n\n    The certify step runs as a standard Step (not ClaudeProcess directly)\n    per spec section 2.5. It uses CERTIFY_GATE for output validation.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    config:\n        RoadmapConfig with output_dir.\n    findings:\n        List of Finding objects to certify. If None, an empty prompt is built.\n    context_sections:\n        Pre-extracted context sections per finding location (NFR-011).\n    \"\"\"\n    from .models import Finding\n\n    out = config.output_dir\n    certification_report = out / \"certification-report.md\"\n\n    prompt = build_certification_prompt(\n        findings=findings or [],\n        context_sections=context_sections or {},\n    )\n\n    return Step(\n        id=\"certify\",\n        prompt=prompt,\n        output_file=certification_report,\n        gate=CERTIFY_GATE,\n        timeout_seconds=300,\n        inputs=[out / \"remediation-tasklist.md\"],\n        retry_limit=1,\n    )\n\n\ndef _build_steps(config: RoadmapConfig) -> list[Step | list[Step]]:\n    \"\"\"Build the 9-step pipeline with parallel generate group.\n\n    Returns a list where each element is either a single Step (sequential)\n    or a list[Step] (parallel group).\n    \"\"\"\n    out = config.output_dir\n\n    # Agent specs\n    agent_a = config.agents[0]\n    agent_b = config.agents[1] if len(config.agents) > 1 else config.agents[0]\n\n    # Output paths\n    extraction = out / \"extraction.md\"\n    roadmap_a = out / f\"roadmap-{agent_a.id}.md\"\n    roadmap_b = out / f\"roadmap-{agent_b.id}.md\"\n    diff_file = out / \"diff-analysis.md\"\n    debate_file = out / \"debate-transcript.md\"\n    score_file = out / \"base-selection.md\"\n    merge_file = out / \"roadmap.md\"\n    test_strat = out / \"test-strategy.md\"\n    spec_fidelity_file = out / \"spec-fidelity.md\"\n\n    # Load retrospective content if configured (missing file handled gracefully)\n    retrospective_content: str | None = None\n    if config.retrospective_file is not None and config.retrospective_file.is_file():\n        try:\n            retrospective_content = config.retrospective_file.read_text(\n                encoding=\"utf-8\"\n            )\n        except OSError:\n            retrospective_content = None\n\n    steps: retrospective_content=retrospective_content,\n            ),\n            output_file=extraction,\n            gate=EXTRACT_GATE,\n            timeout_seconds=300,\n            inputs=[config.spec_file],\n            retry_limit=1,\n        ),\n        # Steps 2a+2b: Generate (parallel)\n        [\n            Step(\n                id=f\"generate-{agent_a.id}\",\n                prompt=build_generate_prompt(agent_a, extraction),\n                output_file=roadmap_a,\n                gate=GENERATE_A_GATE,\n                timeout_seconds=900,\n                inputs=[extraction],\n                retry_limit=1,\n                model=agent_a.model,\n            ),\n            Step(\n                id=f\"generate-{agent_b.id}\",\n    retry_limit=1,\n        ),\n        # Step 4: Debate\n        Step(\n            id=\"debate\",\n            prompt=build_debate_prompt(diff_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, config.depth),\n 5: Score\n        Step(\n            id=\"score\",\n            prompt=build_score_prompt(debate_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b),\n            output_file=score_file,\n            gate=SCORE_GATE,\n            timeout_seconds=300,\n            inputs=[debate_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b],\n            retry_limit=1,\n        ),\n        # Step 6: Merge\n        Step(\n            id=\"merge\",\n timeout_seconds=600,\n            inputs=[config.spec_file, merge_file],\n            retry_limit=1,\n        ),\n        # Step 9: Wiring Verification (section 5.7, shadow mode trailing gate)\n \"wiring-verification.md\",\n            gate=WIRING_GATE,\n            timeout_seconds=60,\n            inputs=[merge_file, spec_fidelity_file],\n            retry_limit=0,\n            gate_mode=GateMode.TRAILING,\n        ),\n    ]\n\n    return steps\n\n\ndef _format_halt_output(results: list[StepResult], config: RoadmapConfig) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Format HALT diagnostic output per spec section 6.2.\"\"\"\n    failed = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n    passed = [r for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.PASS]\n    cancelled = [r for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.CANCELLED]\n\n    if not failed:\n        return \"\"\n\n    fail = failed[-1]\n    step = fail.step\n\n    # Calculate file details if output exists\n    file_info = \"\"\n    if step and step.output_file.exists():\n        content = step.output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        byte_count = len(content.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n        line_count = len(content.splitlines())\n        file_info = f\"  Output size: {byte_count} bytes ({line_count} lines)\\n\"\n    elif step:\n        file_info = \"  Output file: not created\\n\"\n\n    elapsed = f\"{fail.duration_seconds:.0f}s\"\n\n    lines = [\n        f\"ERROR: Roadmap pipeline halted at step '{step.id}' (attempt {fail.attempt}/2)\",\n        f\"  Gate failure: {fail.gate_failure_reason}\",\n        f\"  Output file: {step.output_file}\",\n        file_info.rstrip(),\n        f\"  Step timeout: {step.timeout_seconds}s | Elapsed: {elapsed}\",\n        \"\",\n        f\"Completed steps: {', '.join(f'{r.step.id} (PASS, attempt {r.attempt})' for r in passed) or 'none'}\",\n        f\"Failed step:     {step.id} ({fail.status.value}, attempt {fail.attempt})\",\n    ]\n\n    # Collect skipped steps\n    all_step_ids = _get_all_step_ids(config)\n    executed_ids = {r.step.id for r in results}\n    skipped_ids = [sid for sid in all_step_ids if sid not in executed_ids]\n    if cancelled:\n        skipped_ids = [r.step.id for r in cancelled] + skipped_ids\n\n    if skipped_ids:\n        lines.append(f\"Skipped steps:   {', '.join(skipped_ids)}\")\n\n    lines.extend(\n        [\n            \"\",\n            \"To retry from this step:\",\n            f\"  superclaude roadmap run {config.spec_file} --resume\",\n            \"\",\n            \"To inspect the failing output:\",\n            f\"  cat {step.output_file}\",\n        ]\n    )\n\n    return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\ndef _get_all_step_ids(config: RoadmapConfig) -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"Get all step IDs in pipeline order.\"\"\"\n    agent_a = config.agents[0]\n    agent_b = config.agents[1] if len(config.agents) > 1 else config.agents[0]\n    return [\n        \"extract\",\n        f\"generate-{agent_a.id}\",\n        f\"generate-{agent_b.id}\",\n        \"diff\",\n        \"debate\",\n        \"score\",\n        \"merge\",\n        \"test-strategy\",\n        \"spec-fidelity\",\n        \"wiring-verification\",\n        \"remediate\",\n        \"certify\",\n    ]\n\n\ndef _print_terminal_halt(\n    output_dir: Path,\n    remaining_findings: list,\n    attempt_count: int,\n    spec_patch_budget_exhausted: bool = False,\n    file=None,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Print terminal halt message to stderr with finding details (SC-6).\n\n    Outputs:\n    - Attempt count and remaining failing finding count\n    - Per-finding details (ID, severity, description)\n    - Manual-fix instructions including certification report path and resume command\n\n    Args:\n        output_dir: Pipeline output directory (for certification report path)\n        remaining_findings: List of Finding objects that failed remediation\n        attempt_count: Number of remediation attempts made\n        spec_patch_budget_exhausted: If True, note that spec-patch budget is also exhausted\n        file: Output stream (defaults to sys.stderr)\n    \"\"\"\n    if file is None:\n        file = sys.stderr\n\n    certify_path = output_dir / \"certify.md\"\n\n    lines = [\n        \"\",\n        \"=\" * 72,\n        \"TERMINAL HALT: Remediation budget exhausted\",\n        \"=\" * 72,\n        f\"Attempt count: {attempt_count}\",\n        f\"Remaining failing findings: {len(remaining_findings)}\",\n        \"\",\n    ]\n\n    if remaining_findings:\n        lines.append(\"Failing findings:\")\n        for finding in remaining_findings:\n            severity = getattr(finding, \"severity\", \"UNKNOWN\")\n            description = getattr(finding, \"description\", \"\")\n            finding_id = getattr(finding, \"id\", \"UNKNOWN\")\n            lines.append(f\"  - {finding_id} [{severity}]: {description}\")\n        lines.append(\"\")\n\n    lines.extend(\n        [\n            \"Manual fix required:\",\n            f\"  1. Review certification report: {certify_path}\",\n            \"  2. Fix remaining findings manually\",\n            \"  3. Resume pipeline with:\",\n            f\"     superclaude roadmap run --resume\",\n            \"\",\n        ]\n    )\n\n    if spec_patch_budget_exhausted:\n        lines.extend(\n            [\n                \"Note: Both the remediation budget and the spec-patch cycle budget\",\n                \"are exhausted. Full dual-budget recovery is deferred to v2.26.\",\n                \"# TODO(v2.26): implement dual-budget-exhaustion recovery mechanism\",\n                \"\",\n            ]\n        )\n\n    lines.append(\"=\" * 72)\n\n    print(\"\\n\".join(lines), file=file, flush=True)\n\n\ndef _check_annotate_deviations_freshness(\n    output_dir: Path,\n    roadmap_file: Path,\n    gate_pass_state: dict[str, bool] | None = None,\n) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if spec-deviations.md hash matches current roadmap.md (SC-8).\n\n    Reads roadmap_hash from spec-deviations.md frontmatter and compares\n    against sha256(roadmap.md). Fail-closed for all error conditions.\n\n    On hash mismatch, resets spec-fidelity and deviation-analysis gate\n    pass state to force re-run (FR-084).\n\n    Returns:\n        True if hash matches (fresh), False for any failure or mismatch.\n    \"\"\"\n    spec_deviations = output_dir / \"spec-deviations.md\"\n\n    # Case 1: spec-deviations.md not found\n    if not spec_deviations.exists():\n        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: spec-deviations.md not found\")\n        return False\n\n    # Case 2: Read error\n    try:\n        content = spec_deviations.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    except OSError as e:\n        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: read error for spec-deviations.md: %s\", e)\n        return False\n\n    # Case 3: Empty file\n    if not content.strip():\n        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: spec-deviations.md is empty\")\n        return False\n\n    # Case 4: Corrupt/missing frontmatter\n    stripped = content.lstrip()\n    if not stripped.startswith(\"---\"):\n        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: spec-deviations.md has no frontmatter\")\n        return False\n\n    rest = stripped[3:].lstrip(\"\\n\")\n    end_idx = rest.find(\"\\n---\")\n    if end_idx == -1:\n        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: spec-deviations.md has corrupt frontmatter\")\n        return False\n\n    # Parse roadmap_hash from frontmatter\n    saved_hash: str | None = None\n    for line in rest[:end_idx].splitlines():\n        line = line.strip()\n        if line.startswith(\"roadmap_hash:\"):\n            saved_hash = line.split(\":\", 1)[1].strip()\n            break\n\n    # Case 5: roadmap_hash field missing or empty\n    if not saved_hash:\n        _log.warning(\n            \"Freshness check: roadmap_hash field missing or empty in spec-deviations.md\"\n        )\n        return False\n\n    # Case 6: roadmap.md not found\n    if not roadmap_file.exists():\n        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: roadmap.md not found at %s\", roadmap_file)\n        return False\n\n    # Case 7: roadmap.md read error\n    try:\n        roadmap_content = roadmap_file.read_bytes()\n    except OSError as e:\n        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: read error for roadmap.md: %s\", e)\n        return False\n\n    # Case 8: Hash comparison\n    current_hash = hashlib.sha256(roadmap_content).hexdigest()\n    if saved_hash != current_hash:\n        _log.warning(\n            \"Freshness check: hash mismatch -- saved=%s current=%s\",\n            saved_hash[:12],\n            current_hash[:12],\n        )\n        # FR-084: reset gate pass state for downstream steps\n        if gate_pass_state is not None:\n            for key in (\"spec-fidelity\", \"deviation-analysis\"):\n                if key in gate_pass_state:\n                    gate_pass_state[key] = False\n        return False\n\n    return True\n\n\ndef _check_remediation_budget(\n    output_dir: Path,\n    max_attempts: int = 2,\n    halt_fn: \"Callable[[Path, list, int], None] | None\" = None,\n) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if remediation budget allows another attempt (SC-6).\n\n    Reads remediation_attempts from .roadmap-state.json.\n    If attempts >= max_attempts, calls halt_fn (or _print_terminal_halt)\n    and returns False.\n\n    Non-integer remediation_attempts is coerced to 0 with a WARNING log.\n\n    Args:\n        output_dir: Pipeline output directory containing .roadmap-state.json\n        max_attempts: Maximum allowed remediation attempts (default 2)\n        halt_fn: Optional callable for terminal halt; defaults to _print_terminal_halt\n\n    Returns:\n        True if budget allows another attempt, False if exhausted.\n    \"\"\"\n    state_file = output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n    state = read_state(state_file)\n\n    attempts = 0\n    if state is not None:\n        raw = state.get(\"remediation_attempts\", 0)\n        try:\n            attempts = int(raw)\n        except (ValueError, TypeError):\n            _log.warning(\"Non-integer remediation_attempts %r coerced to 0\", raw)\n            attempts = 0\n\n    if attempts >= max_attempts:\n        if halt_fn is not None:\n            halt_fn(output_dir, [], attempts + 1)\n        else:\n            _print_terminal_halt(\n                output_dir=output_dir,\n                remaining_findings=[],\n                attempt_count=attempts + 1,\n            )\n        return False\n\n    return True\n\n\ndef _print_step_start(step: Step) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Print progress output when a step starts.\"\"\"\n    print(f\"[roadmap] Starting step: {step.id}\", flush=True)\n\n\ndef _print_step_complete(step: Step, result: StepResult) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Print progress output when a step completes.\"\"\"\n    duration = f\"{result.duration_seconds:.0f}s\"\n    if result.status == StepStatus.PASS:\n        print(\n            f\"[roadmap] Step {step.id}  PASS (attempt {result.attempt}, {duration})\",\n            flush=True,\n        )\n    else:\n        print(\n            f\"[roadmap] Step {step.id}  {result.status.value} (attempt {result.attempt}, {duration})\",\n            flush=True,\n        )\n        if result.gate_failure_reason:\n            print(f\"           Reason: {result.gate_failure_reason}\", flush=True)\n\n\ndef _dry_run_output(steps: list[Step | list[Step]]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Print step plan and gate criteria for --dry-run.\"\"\"\n    step_num = 0\n    for entry in steps:\n        if isinstance(entry, list):\n            for s in entry:\n                step_num += 1\n                _print_step_plan(step_num, s, parallel=True)\n        else:\n            step_num += 1\n            _print_step_plan(step_num, entry)\n\n\ndef _print_step_plan(num: int, step: Step, parallel: bool = False) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Print a single step's plan entry.\"\"\"\n    par_label = \" (parallel)\" if parallel else \"\"\n    print(f\"Step {num}{par_label}: {step.id}\")\n    print(f\"  Output: {step.output_file}\")\n    print(f\"  Timeout: {step.timeout_seconds}s\")\n    if step.model:\n        print(f\"  Model: {step.model}\")\n    if step.gate:\n        print(f\"  Gate
